Regarding Abortion Negative 90-26-6

**IV. Recommendations**

1. That **Overture 1** from Northwest Georgia Presbytery (“Revise _BCO_

14-2 to Permit Additional Ruling Elder Representation at the
General Assembly,” p. 581) be answered in the **negative** . _Adopted_

<a id="ga47-p88"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=90 printed_page=88 -->

**Grounds:** The Overture points out the continuing imbalance
between Teaching and Ruling Elder attendance at General Assembly
but will not solve the problem it purports to address. Because many
PCA churches send no Ruling Elders to the Assembly, simply
increasing the number of Ruling Elders a church may send will not
have a significant effect.

2. That **Overture 2** from Northwest Georgia Presbytery (“Encourage

CTS toward Growth and Release from Assembly Oversight,” p. 584)
be answered in the **negative.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:**

- The overture is worded in a way that suggests that the Seminary
wants to leave the denomination, but this is not the case. The
seminary board has stated, as the overtures committee heard
from President Dalbey, that Covenant Seminary emphatically
does not want to be removed from denominational oversight
and, in fact, greatly appreciates all the advantages and
accountability that denominational connection and oversight
provides.

- Covenant Seminary was received into the PCA as a part of
Joining and Receiving and it remaining as a part of the
denomination is a part of the commitments that were made at
Joining and Receiving.

- Covenant Seminary, as reported by President Dalbey, does not
desire to have unordained persons serving as members of its
board.

- All board members must affirm agreement with the Westminster
Standards once a year. Every faculty member must also affirm
agreement with the Westminster Standards yearly. Every faculty
member is approved by a vote of the CTS board, which is
nominated and elected by the General Assembly of the PCA.

- The president of Covenant Seminary has passed examination by
the GA's Theological Examining Committee

- While members of the Overtures Committee are very
appreciative of all our confessional seminaries, having a
denominational seminary is a great blessing and benefit to the
Presbyterian Church in America.

<a id="ga47-p89"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=91 printed_page=89 -->

3. _[Overture 3 (p. 587) was referred by the Stated Clerk to AC.]_

4. That **Overture 4** from Calvary Presbytery (“Declare the Council on

Biblical Manhood & Womanhood’s ‘Nashville Statement’ on
Biblical Sexuality as a Biblically Faithful Declaration,” p. 589) be
answered in the **affirmative.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** This Overture commends the Nashville Statement as a
useful, faithful, accurate, and careful summary of biblical truth. This
Overture instructs the PCA to do what our _Book of Church Order_
instructs us to do: “minister and declare” God’s truth to God’s
people. This overture is also very simple and clear. It’s an excellent
resource that every Sunday school teacher, elder, deacon, member of
the PCA can read, understand, and apply in ministry and
conversation. Lastly this Overture is necessary. It appears that many
commissioners have come to Dallas concerned about the discussion

of sexuality within the PCA. This Overture sends a strong word that
the PCA loves people struggling with same-sex attraction so much
that we are willing to tell them careful, faithful, biblical teaching on
sexuality.

5. That **Overture 5** from Central Indiana Presbytery (“Amend _BCO_

23-1 on Procedures for Dissolving Various Pastoral Relations,”
p. 583) be **referred back to Central Indiana Presbytery without**
**prejudice.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** The Overture does not adequately resolve all the issues it
addresses and is in need of further refinement to be consistent with

other sections of the _BCO_ . The best mechanism for further
refinement is to have the presbytery submit a revised version in the
future.

6. That **Overture 6** from Pacific Northwest Presbytery (“Amend _RAO_ 11

and 13 to Disallow Memorials,” p. 594) be answered in the
**affirmative** [See Partial OC Report, p. 24] _._ _Adopted_

7. That **Overture 7** from Pacific Northwest Presbytery (“Form an Ad

Interim Committee to Address Matters Related to Domestic Abuse,
Domestic Oppression, and Sexual Assault,” p.596) be answered in
the **affirmative as amended** (For OC changes, ~~double strike-~~
~~through~~ for deletions, double underlining for additions). _Adopted_

<a id="ga47-p90"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=92 printed_page=90 -->

**Be it resolved:**

1. That the 47th General Assembly create a seven-man Study

Committee on Domestic Abuse and Sexual Assault tasked

to accomplish the following.
a. The Committee shall prepare an annotated bibliography

of resources the Committee endorses on topics related
to child abuse and sexual assault, domestic abuse and
sexual assault, and domestic oppression. [1 ] The annotated
bibliography should also include pastoral resources for
the care of victims of these sins, as well as ministry and
counsel for those overtaken by these sins.
b. The Committee shall report regarding best practices and

guidelines that could be helpful for elders, Sessions,
Presbyteries, and agencies for protecting against these
sins and for responding to them. However, no practice,
policy, or guideline will be proposed for adoption or
approval. It is simply information, which shall not be
binding or obligatory in any sense.
c. The Committee may recommend to the 48th GA

(Birmingham 2020) any statement(s) it believes would
be prudent and warranted for the Assembly to adopt.
d. ~~The Committee~~ ~~may recommend~~ ~~to the 48th GA any~~

~~revisions or additions to~~ ~~_BCO_~~ ~~Rules of Discipline that~~
~~might help prevent these sins, protect and care for~~
~~victims, procure justice, and lead offenders to~~
~~repentance. Reporting procedure is governed by~~ ~~_RAO_~~
~~12-1.~~

~~_RAO_~~ ~~12-1: ...All recommendations [from permanent~~

~~Committees and Agencies, special committees, and ad~~
~~interim committees of the GA] proposing amendment to~~

1 Rev. Warren Lamb (Th.M., MS, MA) Vancouver Bible Institute & Truth in Love Biblical

Counseling Center
"Domestic oppression is an ongoing pattern of intimidating and domineering behavior

employed by one family member to control one or more other family members. A
superior/inferior, hierarchical power differential is established, maintained, and increased
more and more as time goes on...."
"Domestic oppressors systematically tyrannize, intimidate, threaten, emotionally coerce,

dehumanize, objectify, demean, degrade, manipulate, and bully at least one other person
within the family home - usually their spouse."
https://pastorlamb.com/2018/05/08/domestic-oppression-time-to-talk-about-i
https://www.vancouverbible.com/our-staff.html
https://www.tilbcc.com/biblical-counseling-team1.html

<a id="ga47-p91"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=93 printed_page=91 -->

~~the constitution shall be referred to the Overtures~~

~~Committee for their review and recommendation to the~~

~~General Assembly~~ ~~under the rules governing a~~
~~committee of commissioners as applicable~~ ~~(~~ ~~_RAO_~~ ~~14-~~
~~6.d.-k.; 14-7.c.-d.; 14-9.d.-h.) [Underlining added. See~~
~~also~~ ~~_RAO_~~ ~~15-1.]~~
~~Any proposed constitutional amendments shall also be~~
~~referred to the Committee on Constitutional Business~~

~~for advice. (RAO 8-2.b.3 & b.4)~~
2. The 47th GA authorizes the GA Moderator to appoint the

seven voting members of this Committee, per _RAO_ 9-4 and
_RAO_ 19-1 ( _Robert's Rules_, RONR, 11th ed., pp. 174-175,
pp. 495-496, and p. 579).
a. These members shall be PCA ~~ministers~~ teaching elders

or PCA ruling elders, and the Committee shall include
at least ~~two~~ three of each. (Anyone may suggest names
to the Moderator.)
b. The 47th GA authorizes the Moderator to make his

appointments after the 47th GA adjourns.
3. The 47th GA authorizes the Moderator to appoint others to

serve the Committee as non-voting advisors, regardless of
denominational affiliation or gender. The 47th GA
recommends the Moderator consider appointing, as
advisors, at least two subject-matter experts and at least two

women.
4. ~~Committee budget shall be $15,000. This expense shall be~~

~~included in the Administrative Committee budget.~~
~~Presbyteries,~~ ~~churches,~~ ~~and~~ ~~individuals~~ ~~are~~ ~~highly~~
~~encouraged to contribute funds to the AC, designated for this~~
~~work.~~ ~~(The Pacific NW Presbytery has approved $1,000 to~~
~~that end.)~~ The budget for the Study Committee shall be
$25,000 and that funds be derived from gifts to the AC
designated for that purpose.

**Grounds:** This Overture will form a Study Committee to examine
and make recommendation concerning the application of Scripture
and our Standards to difficult and complex issues raised with regard
to domestic abuse, domestic oppression, and sexual assault. The
Study Committee would also provide resources and practices and
guidelines that could be helpful for elders, Sessions, and
Presbyteries.

<a id="ga47-p92"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=94 printed_page=92 -->

8. That **Overture 8** from Ohio Valley Presbytery (“Amend _BCO_ 14-1.10,

14-1.11, and 14-1.12 to Allow Non-ordained Persons to serve on
Committees and Boards,” p. 601) be answered in the **negative.**
_Adopted_

**Grounds:** This Overture would allow non-ordained men or women,
who have not taken a vow to uphold our Constitution, to have a part
in the governance of the church. Such persons may usefully serve as
giving information and advice, but they, apart from Constitutional
commitment, should not be part of the decision-making process
itself. Further, a substantially similar overture was rejected by the
46 [th] Assembly. The arguments have not changed or become more
persuasive.

9. That **Overture 9** from Platte Valley (“Amend _BCO_ 42-4; 43-2; 43-3

regarding Method and Deadlines of Filing Cases,” p. 608) be
answered in the **affirmative as amended** (For OC changes, ~~double~~
~~strike-through~~ for deletions, double underlining for additions).
_Adopted_

**Therefore** _**,**_ **Platte Valley Presbytery overtures** to amend

_BCO_ 42-4 to read ( ~~strikethrough~~ for deletions;
underlining for new words):

**42-4** . Notice of appeal may be given the court before
its adjournment. Written notice of appeal, with
supporting reasons, shall be filed by the appellant
with both the clerk of the lower court and the clerk of
the higher court, within thirty (30) days of notification
of the last court’s decision. ~~Such filing shall not be~~
~~timely unless the documents are received by the clerk~~
~~of the lower court and the clerk of the higher court~~
~~within thirty (30) days of notification of the last~~
~~court’s decision, except that papers shall be deemed~~
~~filed on the day of mailing if certified, registered or~~
~~express mail of the United States Postal Service or~~
~~any private service where verifying receipt is utilized.~~
~~Neither facsimiles nor e-mail will be allowed for~~
~~purposes of filing. Interested parties should be aware~~
~~that responsibility for such filings rests with them and~~

~~-~~
~~that delays in delivery or non~~ ~~delivery are the sole~~
~~responsibility of the filing party.~~

<a id="ga47-p93"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=95 printed_page=93 -->

Notification of the last court’s decision shall be

deemed to have occurred on the day of mailing (if
certified, registered or express mail of a national
postal service or any private service where verifying
receipt is utilized), the day of hand delivery, or the
day of confirmed receipt in the case of e-mail or
facsimile. Furthermore, compliance with such
requirements shall be deemed to have been fulfilled if
a party cannot be located after diligent inquiry or if a
party refuses to accept delivery. No attempt should be
made to circularize the courts to which appeal is being
made by either party before the case is heard.

So that it reads:

with both the clerk of the lower court and the clerk of

the higher court, within thirty (30) days of notification
of the last court’s decision. ~~Such filing shall not be~~
~~timely unless the documents are received by the clerk~~

**And, therefore** _**,**_ **Platte Valley Presbytery further overtures**

to amend _BCO_ 43-2 to read ( ~~strikethrough~~ for deletions;
underlining for new words):

**43-2** . A complaint shall first be made to the court
whose act or decision is alleged to be in error. Written
notice of complaint, with supporting reasons, shall be
filed with the clerk of the court within sixty (60) days
following the meeting of the court. ~~Such filing shall~~
~~not be timely unless the documents are received in the~~
~~office of the Stated Clerk within sixty (60) days~~
~~following the meeting of the court, except that papers~~
~~shall be deemed filed on the day of mailing if~~
~~certified, registered or express mail of the United~~
~~States Postal Service or any private service where~~

~~-~~
~~verifying receipt is utilized. Neither facsimiles nor e~~
~~mail will be allowed for purposes of filing. In regard~~
~~to filing a complaint with a court, as outlined in this~~
~~section, or in taking that complaint to a higher court,~~
~~as outlined in~~ ~~_BCO_~~ ~~43-3, interested parties should be~~
~~aware that responsibility for such filings rests with~~
~~them and that delays in delivery or nondelivery are~~
~~the sole responsibility of the filing party.~~ The court
shall consider the complaint at its next stated meeting,
or at a called meeting prior to its next stated meeting.
No attempt should be made to circularize the court to
which complaint is being made by either party.

So that it reads:

**43-2** . A complaint shall first be made to the court
whose act or decision is alleged to be in error. Written
notice of complaint, with supporting reasons, shall be
filed with the clerk of the court within sixty (60) days
following the meeting of the court. ~~Such filing shall~~

**And, therefore**, Platte Valley Presbytery further overtures

to amend _BCO_ 43-3 to read ( ~~strikethrough~~ for deletions;
underlining for new words):

**43-3** . If, after considering a complaint, the court
alleged to be delinquent or in error is of the opinion
that it has not erred, and denies the complaint, the
complainant may take that complaint to the next
higher court. If the lower court fails to consider the
complaint against it by or at its next stated meeting,
the complainant may take that complaint to the next
higher court. Written notice thereof shall be filed with
both the clerk of the lower court and the clerk of the

higher court within thirty (30) days of notification of
the last court’s decision. ~~Such filing shall not be~~
~~timely unless the documents are received by the clerk~~
~~of the lower court and the clerk of the higher court~~
~~within thirty (30) days of notification of the last~~
~~court’s decision, except that papers shall be deemed~~
~~filed on the day of mailing if certified, registered or~~
~~express mail of the United States Postal Service or~~
~~any private service where verifying receipt is utilized.~~

~~purposes of filing.~~

deemed to have occurred on the day of mailing (if
certified, registered or express mail of a national
postal service or any private service where verifying
receipt is utilized), the day of hand delivery, or the
day of confirmed receipt in the case of e-mail or
facsimile. Furthermore, compliance with such
requirements shall be deemed to have been fulfilled if
a party cannot be located after diligent inquiry or if a
party refuses to accept delivery.

So that it reads:

**43-3** . If, after considering a complaint, the court
alleged to be delinquent or in error is of the opinion
that it has not erred, and denies the complaint, the
complainant may take that complaint to the next
higher court. If the lower court fails to consider the
complaint against it by or at its next stated meeting,
the complainant may take that complaint to the next
higher court. Written notice thereof shall be filed with
both the clerk of the lower court and the clerk of the

higher court within thirty (30) days of notification of

Notification of the last court’s decision shall be

deemed to have occurred on the day of mailing (if
certified, registered or express mail of a national
postal service or any private service where verifying

day of confirmed receipt in the case of e-mail or

facsimile. Furthermore, compliance with such
requirements shall be deemed to have been fulfilled if
a party cannot be located after diligent inquiry or if a

**Grounds:** The amended Overture gives helpful clarifying language
that it is the notification of the last court’s decision that starts the

running of the clock on complaints and appeals without definitively
prohibiting electronic filings to all courts.

Distribute RPCNA’s ‘Contemporary Perspectives on Sexual Orientation:

**affirmative as amended.** (OC changes: ~~double strike-through~~ for
deletions, double underlining for additions) **.** _Adopted_

**Therefore, be it resolved** that the General Assembly

commend _The Gospel & Sexual Orientation_
(available in the PCA Bookstore and online) to the
presbyteries and congregations of the denomination
as a work consistent with our standards and helpful to
answer questions arising in the culture regarding
same-sex attraction and how the church should

minister to those who struggle with this sin.

~~**Therefore, be it resolved**~~ ~~that the General Assembly~~

~~receive “Contemporary Perspectives on Sexual~~
~~Orientation: A Theological and Pastoral Analysis”~~
~~(available~~ ~~on~~ ~~pages~~ ~~86-131~~ ~~at~~
~~[http://rparchives.org/data/Minutes%20of%20Synod/](https://na01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=http%3A%2F%2Frparchives.org%2Fdata%2FMinutes%2520of%2520Synod%2F2011%2520Minutes.pdf&data=02%7C01%7Cac%40pcanet.org%7C5c55a37c455d47c4f64108d68c9979df%7C0f18fcd7edb64993b4d03112a12a45f4%7C0%7C0%7C636850988138791726&sdata=FHOPUgzD%2Bq2OwE09u9gMJi59QrKVTJPXDqyGdCryCh4%3D&reserved=0)~~
~~[2011%20Minutes.pdf) and distribute it to the](https://na01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=http%3A%2F%2Frparchives.org%2Fdata%2FMinutes%2520of%2520Synod%2F2011%2520Minutes.pdf&data=02%7C01%7Cac%40pcanet.org%7C5c55a37c455d47c4f64108d68c9979df%7C0f18fcd7edb64993b4d03112a12a45f4%7C0%7C0%7C636850988138791726&sdata=FHOPUgzD%2Bq2OwE09u9gMJi59QrKVTJPXDqyGdCryCh4%3D&reserved=0)~~
~~presbyteries and congregations of the denomination;~~
~~and~~

~~**Be it further resolved**~~ ~~that the General Assembly direct~~

~~the Committee on Discipleship Ministries to publish~~
~~the CPSO for sale and distribution, along with the~~

<a id="ga47-p98"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=100 printed_page=98 -->

~~other actions of the Assembly related to human~~
~~sexuality, especially the actions of the 5~~ ~~[t][h]~~ ~~General~~
~~Assembly and the Joining and Receiving, as the~~
~~PCA’s statements on the Gospel and human~~
~~sexuality; and~~

~~**Be it further resolved**~~ ~~that the General Assembly direct~~

~~the Committee on Discipleship Ministries to assist~~
~~sessions in the watching over their flock and in the~~
~~nourishing and guarding of the children of the church~~
~~by creating a resource or curriculum based on the~~
~~CPSO report that could be used with students that~~
~~might be struggling with issues related to sexual~~
~~identity, same sex attraction, or gender confusion; and~~

~~**Be it further resolved**~~ ~~that the General Assembly direct~~

~~Reformed University Fellowship to participate in~~
~~promoting the prosperity and enlargement of the~~
~~Church by incorporating CPSO in their annual~~
~~training; and~~

~~**Be it further resolved**~~ ~~that the General Assembly direct~~

~~Covenant College to nourish and safeguard the young~~
~~people of the church by making this report available~~
~~to faculty, staff, and students, as well as including the~~
~~content of this report in the life of the school in some~~
~~fashion (perhaps through RA training, chapel, or~~
~~other venues); and~~

~~**Be it further resolved**~~ ~~that the General Assembly~~

~~encourage Sessions and elders to be active in~~
~~shepherding those in their flock that struggle against~~
~~same sex attraction or sexual confusion so that elders~~

~~might keep watch over their flocks as ones who will~~
~~give an account, and that we all might win a full~~
~~reward (Heb. 13:17; 2 John 8).~~

**Grounds:** This Overture draws on the wisdom of our NAPARC
cousins, the RPCNA, who have produced an excellent analysis of
contemporary perspectives on sexual orientations. The concerns
raised by AC and CDM committees have been addressed by
amendments and this document will assist the church in its

<a id="ga47-p99"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=101 printed_page=99 -->

understanding and pastoral care. The RPCNA report is basically in
two parts. The first part is focused on the biblical and theological
understanding of the issue of sexual orientation and gender. The
second part gives many practical and most importantly pastoral
suggestions in how to gently and winsomely minister to those who
struggle.

17. That **Overture 17** from Houston Metro Presbytery (“Amend _BCO_

32-8 to Allow for Video Testimony by Witnesses,” p. 629) be
answered in the **affirmative as amended** (For OC changes, ~~double~~
~~strike-through~~ for deletions, double underlining for additions).
_Adopted_

**Therefore, be it resolved**, that the General Assembly of the

Presbyterian Church in America amend _BCO_ 32-8,
32-13 and 35-10 with the following (additions are
underlined, deletions are struck through):
**32-8** . When the offense with which an accused person
stands charged took place at a distance, ~~and or~~ and it

<a id="ga47-p100"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=102 printed_page=100 -->

is inconvenient for the witnesses to appear before the
court having jurisdiction, that court may either (a)
appoint a commission of its body, or (b) request the
coordinate court contiguous to the place where the
facts occurred, to take the testimony for it, or (c) have
the testimony taken by videoconference, which shall
employ technical means that ensure that all persons
participating in the meeting can see and hear each
other at the same time, and which allows for live
cross-examination by both parties. The accused shall
always have reasonable notice of the time and place
of the meeting of this commission or coordinate court.

**32-13** . In order that the trial may be fair and
impartial, the witnesses shall be examined in the
presence of the accused (as permitted by _BCO_ 32-8),
or at least after he shall have received due citation to

attend. Witnesses may be cross-examined by both
parties, and any questions asked must be pertinent to
the issue.

**35-10** . When it is not convenient for a court to have

the whole or perhaps any part of the testimony in any
particular case taken in its presence, a commission
shall be appointed, or coordinate court requested, to
take the testimony in question, which shall be
considered as if taken in the presence of the court.

Due notice of the commission or coordinate court or

videoconference, and of the time and place of its
meeting, shall be given to the opposite party, that he
may have an opportunity of attending. If the accused
shall desire on his part to take testimony at a distance
for his own exculpation, he shall give notice to the
court of the time and place at which it shall be taken,
in order that a commission or coordinate court, as in
the former case, may be appointed for the purpose.
Testimony may be taken on written interrogatories by
filing the same with the clerk of the court having
jurisdiction of the case, and giving two weeks’ notice
thereof to the adverse party, during which time he

<a id="ga47-p101"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=103 printed_page=101 -->

may file cross-interrogatories, if he desire it. Testimony
shall then be taken by the commission or coordinate
court in answer to the direct and cross-interrogatories,
if such are filed, and no notice need be given of the
time and place of taking the testimony.

**Grounds:** The Overture gives courts the option to use modern
technology to take testimony from witnesses, in addition to creating
a commission or using a coordinate court. The amendments to this
overture address the concerns of CCB about consistency in
application in two other sections of the BCO.

33. That **Overture 33** from Tennessee Valley Presbytery (“Amend

_BCO_ 57-5 to Add Vow Regarding Trinity,” p. 670) be answered in
the **negative.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** The current membership vows are sufficient and include
references to each Person of the Trinity.

34. That **Overture 34 (** _BCO_ amendments only) from Philadelphia

Presbytery (“Amend _BCO_ 42-4; 43-2; and 43-3 as well as _OMSJC_
18.10 b. to Clarify Filing Requirements and Allow Electronic
Filings,” p. 672) be **referred back to Philadelphia Presbytery**
**without prejudice.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** The Overture addresses important issues of what
constitutes (and needs to be included or not included in) a complaint

<a id="ga47-p103"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=105 printed_page=103 -->

to a higher court, and the requirement of simultaneous filing.
However, the language of the Overture is overly complex and can be
perfected. The best mechanism for further refinement is to have the
presbytery submit a revised version in the future.

35. That **Overture 35** from Savannah River Presbytery (“Affirm the

PCA’s Commitment to the Sanctity of Biblical Sexual Relationships,”
p. 677) be **ruled out of order by the GA Moderator.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** This Overture is out of order because it attempts to
reaffirm a position previously taken. Such a motion serves no useful
purpose because the original motion is still in effect; also, possible
attempts to amend motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with
the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted;
and if such a motion to reaffirm failed, it would create an ambiguous
situation [ _RONR_ (11th ed.) p. 104, ll.24-31].

36. That **Overture 36** from Savannah River Presbytery (“Amend _BCO_

40-4 and 40-5 To Clarify the Meaning of ‘Credible Report,’” p. 684)
be answered in the **negative.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** _BCO_ 40-5 is an extremely complicated provision and
needs clarification beyond that presented in the Overture. The
Overture also unnecessarily limits what a credible report could be.

37. That **Overture 37** from James River Presbytery (“Assembly

Statement on Same-sex Attraction,” p. 687) be answered **with**
**reference to Overture 4.** _Adopted_

38. That **Overture 38** from Georgia Foothills Presbytery (“Establish a

Study Committee on Domestic Violence and Sexual Assault,”
p. 689) be answered **with reference to Overture 7.** _Adopted_

39. That **Overture 39** from Southern New England Presbytery (“Re
commend the Previous Statements and Study Reports of the PCA in
Regard to Homosexuality,” p. 691) be **ruled out of order by the GA**
**Moderator.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** This Overture is out of order because it attempts to
reaffirm a position previously taken. Such a motion serves no useful
purpose because the original motion is still in effect; also, possible

<a id="ga47-p104"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=106 printed_page=104 -->

attempts to amend motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with
the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted;
and if such a motion to reaffirm failed, it would create an ambiguous
situation [ _RONR_ (11th ed.) p. 104, ll.24-31].

40. That **Overture 40** from Northwest Georgia Presbytery (“Overture

for Sessions to Acknowledge and Support Women Leaders without
Delay or More Divisiveness,” p. 692) be answered in the **negative.**
_Adopted_

**Grounds:** This Overture is too vague a resolution (“be authorized”)
to a vague problem (“churches wishing to acknowledge”) that would
create confusion around the doctrine of ordination (“with the laying
on of hands”).

41. _[Overture 41 p. (693) was referred by the Stated Clerk to MTW.]_

42. That **Overture 42** from Chicago Metro Presbytery (“Establish Study

Committee on Sexuality,” p. 701) be answered in the **affirmative as**
**amended** (For OC changes, ~~double strike-through~~ for deletions,
double underlining for additions). _Adopted_

**Therefore, be it resolved** that the 47 [th ] General Assembly

create an Ad-Interim Committee (AIC) to study the
topic of human sexuality with particular attention to the
issues of homosexuality, same-sex attraction, and
transgenderism, and prepare a report which:

- Shall include an annotated bibliography of resources
on sexuality which the Committee endorses to help
pastors and sessions shepherd congregants who are
dealing with same-sex attraction; and

- Shall address: (1) the nature of temptation, sin, ~~and~~
repentance, and the difference between Roman
Catholic and Reformed views of concupiscence as
regards same sex- attraction; (2) the propriety of
using terms like “gay Christian” when referring to
a believer struggling with same-sex attraction; (3)
the status of "orientation" as a valid anthropological
category; (4) the practice of “spiritual friendship”
among same sex attracted Christians; and

<a id="ga47-p105"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=107 printed_page=105 -->

- Shall include an analysis of WLC 138 and 139
regarding same-sex attraction, with careful
attention given to the compatibility of the 7 [th]
commandment and same-sex attraction and the

pursuit of celibacy by those who are attracted to the
same sex, and

- Shall include exegesis of the terms “ _malakoi_ ” and
“ _arsenokoitai_ ” (1 Cor. 6:9); and

- Shall include suggested ways to articulate and
defend a Biblical understanding of homosexuality,
same sex-attraction, and transgenderism in the
context of a culture that denies that understanding;
and

- Should the Committee conclude it prudent, may, as
one of its recommendations, propose a statement on
the topic; and

**Be it further resolved,** that, in its examination of Scripture and

the PCA _of Faith_ and Catechisms, the Committee consider
studies and statements of other bodies outside and within

the PCA (including, but not limited to, the Council on
Biblical Manhood and Womanhood’s “Nashville Statement”;
the RPCNA’s “Contemporary _Confession_ Perspectives on
Sexual Orientation: A Theological and Pastoral Analysis”;
the RPCES’ “Pastoral Care for the Repentant Homosexual”;
Missouri Presbytery’s “Homosexuality and the Gospel of
Grace: Faithfulness to the Lord’s calling in an age of
Sexual Autonomy”; the Central Carolina Presbytery Study
Committee Report on 2018 Revoice Conference; the 2019
Missouri Presbytery Report on the Revoice Conference;
the statements on homosexuality issued by the 5 [th], 24 [th], and
27 [th ] General Assemblies of the PCA and the January 14, 2015,
Report of the CMC Subcommittee on Homosexuality) and
resources recommended in those documents; and

**Be it further resolved** that the Moderator of the 47 [th ] General

Assembly appoint the seven voting members who shall be
either PCA teaching or PCA ruling elders, and the
Committee shall include at least ~~two~~ three teaching and ~~two~~
three ruling elders ~~including members whose writings~~
~~reflect theological engagement on the issue and pastoral~~

<a id="ga47-p106"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=108 printed_page=106 -->

~~interaction with those dealing with same sex attraction and~~
~~transgenderism;~~ and

**Be it further resolved** that the Committee be permitted to

recruit others to serve the Committee as advisors within the

confines of its budget, with particular concern to secure
advisors with experience in counseling those dealing with
same-sex attraction and transgenderism; and

**Be it further resolved** that the budget for the study

committee be set at $15,000/year and that funds be
derived from gifts to the AC designated for that
purpose; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall present its

report to the 48 [th ] General Assembly, including any
recommendations it may present.

~~1~~ ~~The Ohio presbytery version here reads “that in addition~~
~~to Scripture and the PCA~~ ~~_Confession of Faith_~~ ~~and~~
~~Catechisms….” Trinity’s Session made the small~~
~~amendment to “in its examination of…”~~

**Grounds:**

- As of today, the PCA is not unified in how it should respond to
present-day controversies surrounding human sexuality. While
there is little, if any, debate within the PCA over the Scripture's
teaching on sexual immorality, there are differences in what
written statements we should make about it as a denomination.

In cases of such controversy, a study committee is helpful in
clarifying what all perspectives can affirm while articulating the
more controversial views with nuance and sensitivity.

- While there is considerable consensus among presbyters on the
Biblical doctrine of human sexuality, there are different opinions
on how best to pastor those struggling with homosexual sin and
temptation. A study committee provides the opportunity to offer
guidance and resources on such counseling.

- Different presbyteries have proposed different statements of
affirmation and denials regarding human sexuality, each
emphasizing different aspects of the denomination's response to

<a id="ga47-p107"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=109 printed_page=107 -->

the issues. The different views coming from the different
presbyteries should be studied and resolved.

- None of the statements recommended by the overtures
committee include all of the pious advice that our churches and
ministers are requesting. a study committee is a time-honored
way to present a unified and consistent report and advice
regarding these issues.

43. That **Overture 43** from Metro Atlanta Presbytery (“Formation of a

Study Committee on Domestic Abuse and Sexual Assault,” p. 703)
be answered **with reference to Overture 7.** _Adopted_

44. That **Overture 44** from Ohio Valley Presbytery (“Establish Ad

Interim Committee to Study Human Sexuality,” p. 705) be answered
**with reference to Overture 42.** _Adopted_

45. That **Overture 45** from Missouri Presbytery (“Form an Ad Interim

Committee to Seek Consensus on Doctrinal Boundaries and Pastoral

Care in the Current Debates About Sexuality,” p.707) be answered
**with reference to Overture 42.** _Adopted_

46. That **Overture 46** from Hills and Plains Presbytery (“Reaffirm

PCA’s Commitment Regarding the Sanctity of Life and Our Stance
Against Abortion in Particular,” p. 709) be **ruled out of order by**
**the GA Moderator.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** This Overture is out of order because it attempts to
reaffirm a position previously taken. Such a motion serves no useful
purpose because the original motion is still in effect; also, possible
attempts to amend motion to reaffirm would come into conflict with
the rules for the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted;
and if such a motion to reaffirm failed, it would create an ambiguous
situation [ _RONR_ (11th ed.) p. 104, ll.24-31].

47. That **Overture 47** from Missouri Presbytery (“Formation of a Study

Committee on Domestic Abuse and Sexual Assault,” p. 711) be
answered **with reference to Overture 7.** _Adopted_

48. That **Overture 48** from the Session of Christ the King Presbyterian

Church, Norman, Oklahoma (Rejected by Hills and Plains
Presbytery at its stated meeting May 4, 2019 [ _RAO_ 11-10]) (“Make

<a id="ga47-p108"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=110 printed_page=108 -->

a Stronger Statement regarding Abortion,” p. 712) be answered in
the **negative.** _Adopted_

**Grounds:** The PCA has spoken biblically, clearly, and pastorally on
the issue of abortion. For reference, see:
1979–Overture 7-Revise Assembly’s statement on abortion
1980–Overture 12-Reaffirm original statement on abortion
1984–Overture 14-Adopt statement that a memorial service is Biblical
and appropriate in cases of miscarriage
1989–Overture 17-Oppose research on transplantation of fetal tissue
1993–Overture 25-Protection of Life of the Unborn
1993–Overture 41-Exhort SBC to Continue Strong Stand Against
Abortion

1999–Overture 27-Reaffirm the sanctity of life
2010–Overture 28-Sanctity of Life Resolution
2014–Overture 43-Expression of Support for Sanctity of Life and
Marriage

**OVERTURES COMMITTEE**

**MINORITY REPORTS (OVERTURES 4, 8, 28, 37)**

**MINORITY REPORT – OVERTURE 4**

If Overture 42 has been answered in the affirmative prior to this report, then
the minority will bring the following substitute motion:

**Substitute Motion** : That Overture 4 be answered in reference to the
Assembly’s action on Overture 42.

If this report comes to the floor prior to Overture 42, then the minority will
bring the following substitute: [Clerk’s Note: Overture 4 was adopted before
Overture 42 was taken up.]

**Substitute Motion** : That Overture 4 from Calvary Presbytery recommitted
to the Overtures Committee of the 48 [th] General Assembly.

Rationale:

1. The minority believes that the Nashville Statement should be a part

of the material studied by a General Assembly Study Committee. A
study committee is afar better way to make a clear and decisive
statement on the urgent issue of human sexuality.
2. Overture 4 only asks the Assembly to “declare…the ‘Nashville

Statement’… as a biblically faithful declaration.” It does not make it
an official document of the PCA. We need a stronger statement by
our denomination and a study committee can do this. A study

<a id="ga47-p113"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=115 printed_page=113 -->

committee report would be a part of our official documents given to
all of our presbyteries, churches, and ministers as pious advice. We
need such a resource as a study committee made up of some of our
best can produce.
3. The Nashville Statement does not address the issue of pastoral care.

Our denomination needs a strong biblical, theological, and pastoral
statement on this topic.

Signed

RE Jim Wert TE Kevin Twit TE Tag Tuck
RE David Nok Daniel TE Tim LeCroy TE Stan McMahan
RE Trevor Laurence TE Giorgio Hirtt TE Dan Herron
RE Matt Teeselink TE Walter Heneger TE Wayne Larson

**MINORITY REPORT – OVERTURE 8**

The Minority moves the following as a substitute motion: That **Overture 8**
be answered in the **AFFIRMATIVE** as amended here:

**Therefore be it resolved** to amend the _Book of Church Order_ by

deleting 14-1.10; by amending 14.1.11 as follows (and
renumbering as 14-1.10) by adding a new 14-1.11; and by
amending 14-12 as follows: [Proposed additions underlined and
deletions noted by strike out.]

_**BCO**_ **14-1**
~~10. The committees are to be established on the basis of an equal~~
~~number between of teaching elders, and ruling elders.~~

<a id="ga47-p114"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=116 printed_page=114 -->

~~11 1~~ 0. A Nominating Committee shall be comprised of one
representative elected by each Presbytery in the following
manner. Each Presbytery shall be assigned to a class by the
stated clerk based on its date of formation. The members shall

serve in classes of three year terms, alternating between ruling
and teaching elders. When necessary, unexpired terms shall be
filled by an elder of the same class, teaching or ruling.
This committee is to present all nominations for which it is
responsible to the next meeting of the Assembly from a slate of
~~men~~ candidates nominated by the Presbyteries. Presbyteries
shall utilize the nominating forms provided by the stated clerk
for their nominations. Each presbytery may present one teaching
elder, ~~and~~ one ruling elder, and, when applicable, one candidate
who is not an elder to fill openings that are designated for
members at large for each committee or agency.
In addition to nominees for expired terms, the Committee
shall nominate for each permanent Committee one ruling, ~~and~~
one teaching elder and a member at large candidate as alternates
to fill any vacancies that may occur during the year. Each
alternate should attend each meeting and fill any vacancy
necessary to meet a quorum. In addition to the new nominees
from the Presbyteries, alternates not assuming any vacancies
during a year will be automatically considered by the
Nominating Committee as candidates for nomination to that
same committee.

11. To be qualified for nomination and election to serve on a

Permanent Committee or Agency as a member at large, a non
ordained candidate must be a member in good standing of a
Church in the Presbyterian Church in America and must receive
an endorsement from their Session. The endorsement will
consist of testimony regarding the candidate’s Christian
character, knowledge of the Bible, knowledge of and assent to
the Constitution of the Church, and promise of faithful service
to the General Assembly.

12 ~~. T~~ he Assembly permanent committees are the
Administrative Committee of the General Assembly,
Committee on Discipleship Ministries, Committee on Mission
to North America, Committee on Mission to the World, and
Committee on Reformed University Fellowship.

<a id="ga47-p115"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=117 printed_page=115 -->

The Administrative Committee of the General Assembly
(AC) shall consist of twenty-one ( ~~20~~ 21) members:

a. ~~Eleven~~ Twelve members shall be in classes elected

through the standard nomination and election
procedure,
b. One ordained member from each of the following

program committees or agencies:
1. Committee on Discipleship Ministries (CDM)
2. Covenant College (CC)
3. Covenant Theological Seminary (CTS)
4. Mission to North America (MNA)
5. Mission to the World (MTW)
6. PCA Foundation (PCAF)
7. PCA Retirement & Benefits, Inc. (RBI)
8. Reformed University Fellowship (RUF)
9. Ridge Haven Conference Center (RH)
The ~~eleven~~ twelve elected members shall be divided into

four classes of three members. Each class will consist of one TE,
one RE, and one member at large, and shall serve a term of four
years. ~~at large shall serve a term of four years.~~ The chairman of
the Administrative Committee shall be one of its elected

ordained members ~~at large~~ .
Each program committee and agency shall designate its
member each year at the last meeting of the committee or board
before the meeting of the General Assembly. The chief
administrative officers of the program committees and agencies
may attend any meeting of the Administrative Committee. They
shall be entitled to the privilege of the floor but shall not have a
vote and must be excluded when an executive session is called.

Committee on Discipleship Ministries, Committee on
Mission to North America, Committee on Mission to the World,
and Committee on Reformed University Fellowship shall
consist of fifteen (15) ~~men~~ members divided into five classes of
three ~~men~~ members ~~each, with two men Being TEs and one RE~~
~~or two men being REs and one TE on alternate years,~~ Each class
will consist of one TE, one RE, and one member at large, and
shall be elected to serve five-year terms. Committee on
Discipleship Ministries, Committee on Mission to North
America, Committee on Mission to the World, and Committee
on Reformed University Fellowship shall have one ruling elder,
~~and~~ one teaching elder and one member at large as alternates to

<a id="ga47-p116"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=118 printed_page=116 -->

fill any vacancy that may occur during the year. A quorum for
each Committee shall be eight (8) members, with a majority of
those present being elders. The Chairman of each Committee
shall be an elder.

Persons who have served for a full term, or for at least two
years of a partial term, on one of the Assembly’s permanent
committees or agencies shall not be eligible for re-election to an
Assembly committee until one year has lapsed. (Exceptions may
be permitted in agency bylaws approved by the Assembly).

**So that** _**BCO**_ **14-1.10, 14-1.11 and 14-1.12 as amended would read:**

10. A Nominating Committee shall be comprised of one
representative elected by each Presbytery in the following
manner. Each Presbytery shall be assigned to a class by the
stated clerk based on its date of formation. The members shall

serve in classes of three year terms, alternating between ruling
and teaching elders. When necessary, unexpired terms shall be
filled by an elder of the same class, teaching or ruling.
This committee is to present all nominations for which it is
responsible to the next meeting of the Assembly from a slate of
candidates nominated by the Presbyteries. Presbyteries shall
utilize the nominating forms provided by the stated clerk for
their nominations. Each presbytery may present one teaching
elder, one ruling elder, and, when applicable, one candidate who
is not an elder to fill openings that are designated for members
at large for each committee or agency.
In addition to nominees for expired terms, the Committee
shall nominate for each permanent Committee one ruling, one
teaching elder and a member at large candidate as alternates to
fill any vacancies that may occur during the year. Each alternate
should attend each meeting and fill any vacancy necessary to
meet a quorum. In addition to the new nominees from the
Presbyteries, alternates not assuming any vacancies during a
year will be automatically considered by the Nominating
Committee as candidates for nomination to that same

committee.

11. To be qualified for nomination and election to serve on a
Permanent Committee or Agency as a member at large, anon ordained candidate must be a member in good standing of a

<a id="ga47-p117"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=119 printed_page=117 -->

Church in the Presbyterian Church in America and must receive
an endorsement from their Session. The endorsement will
consist of testimony regarding the candidate’s Christian
character, knowledge of the Bible, knowledge of and assent to
the Constitution of the Church, and promise of faithful service
to the General Assembly.

12. The Assembly permanent committees are the
Administrative Committee of the General Assembly,
Committee on Discipleship Ministries, Committee on Mission
to North America, Committee on Mission to the World, and
Committee on Reformed University Fellowship.
The Administrative Committee of the General Assembly
(AC) shall consist of twenty-one (21) members:

a. Twelve members shall be in classes elected through the

standard nomination and election procedure,
b. One ordained member from each of the following

program committees or agencies:
1. Committee on Discipleship Ministries (CDM)
2. Covenant College (CC)
3. Covenant Theological Seminary (CTS)
4. Mission to North America (MNA)
5. Mission to the World (MTW)
6. PCA Foundation (PCAF)
7. PCA Retirement & Benefits, Inc. (RBI)
8. Reformed University Fellowship (RUF)
9. Ridge Haven Conference Center (RH)
The twelve elected members shall be divided into four

classes of three members. Each class will consist of one TE, one
RE, and one member at large, and shall serve a term of four
years. The chairman of the Administrative Committee shall be
one of its elected ordained members.

Each program committee and agency shall designate its
member each year at the last meeting of the committee or board
before the meeting of the General Assembly. The chief
administrative officers of the program committees and agencies
may attend any meeting of the Administrative Committee. They
shall be entitled to the privilege of the floor but shall not have a
vote and must be excluded when an executive session is called.

Committee on Discipleship Ministries, Committee on
Mission to North America, Committee on Mission to the World,

<a id="ga47-p118"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=120 printed_page=118 -->

and Committee on Reformed University Fellowship shall
consist of fifteen (15) members divided into five classes of three
members. Each class will consist of one TE, one RE, and one
member at large, and shall be elected to serve five-year terms.
Committee on Discipleship Ministries, Committee on Mission
to North America, Committee on Mission to the World, and
Committee on Reformed University Fellowship shall have one
ruling elder, one teaching elder and one member at large as
alternates to fill any vacancy that may occur during the year. A
quorum for each Committee shall be eight (8) members, with a
majority of those present being elders. The Chairman of each
Committee shall be an elder.

Persons who have served for a full term, or for at least two
years of a partial term, on one of the Assembly’s permanent
committees or agencies shall not be eligible for re-election to an
Assembly committee until one year has lapsed. (Exceptions may
be permitted in agency bylaws approved by the Assembly).

Grounds:

We believe that this overture affirms the gifts that have been distributed to
the saints in the Church, (Romans 12:4-8, I Corinthians 12: 4-31) while
affirming the “spiritual commonwealth” ( _BCO 3-1_ ) in which members elect
godly men to be ordained as elders who are given the power of jurisdiction
as they act jointly in the courts of the church. ( _BCO 3-2_ ).

Approving this overture will not place non-ordained men and women in
positions of authority that is to be reserved exclusively for ordained men.
This overture allows for non-ordained men and women to serve on the

_committees_ of the church. It is important to add emphasis to the word
_committee_ . Unlike commissions, committees are appointed by courts and are
limited in that they are only permitted “to examine, consider, and report’ to
their constituting court regarding the matters that the court delegates to them
( _BCO 15-1_ ). They do not exercise governing authority. The Permanent
Committees of the General Assembly are subject to additional Constitutional
constraint in that they are established “to serve and not direct any Church
judicatories. They are not to establish policy, but rather execute policy
established by the General Assembly”. ( _BCO 14-1.7_ )

This overture is written to give confidence that the non-ordained men and
women that are placed in nomination for service on these committees are

<a id="ga47-p119"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=121 printed_page=119 -->

qualified to serve. Each candidate must first be a member in good standing.
All members in good standing are accountable to their sessions having taken
a vow to submit themselves “to the government and discipline of the church”
( _BCO 57.5_ ). Additionally, each candidate will be required to get an
endorsement from their session that gives testimony to the nominee’s
Christian character, their knowledge of and assent to the Constitution of the
Church and their promise of faithful service to the General Assembly. Just
as our courts serve the Church well by training, examining and holding
officers accountable, we anticipate that sessions and presbyteries will be
diligent in preparing, examining and holding accountable those that will be
nominated and chosen to serve the General Assembly.

Finally, we would note the conflict with the _RAO_ that would be created were
this overture to be approved. However, the _RAO_ is subordinate to the _BCO_
and any conflicts would be resolved in favor of the _BCO_ . Further, if the
overture is approved, subsequent assemblies can approve changes to the _RAO_
that would bring the _RAO_ into compliance with the _BCO_ .

In summary, this overture will demonstrates our high view of
complementarianism by recognizing the gifts and abilities of all of our
members, men and women, while avoiding the errors of egalitarianism by
correctly assigning distinct roles and responsibilities to the ordained officers
of the church.

We would respectfully urge the commissioners of the 47 [th] General Assembly
to answer this overture in the affirmative.

RE David Nok Daniel Southern New England
TE Jeremy Fair Hills and Plains
TE Kyle Hackmann Eastern Canada
TE Dan Herron Central Indiana

TE James Kessler Columbus Metro

TE Benjamin Lyon Tidewater
RE E. J. Nusbaum Rocky Mountain
RE Nathan Partain Central Indiana

TE Sean Roberts Northland New England
TE Scott Seaton Potomac

RE Bruce Terrell Metro New York

TE Doug Tharp Eastern Pennsylvania
TE Kevin Twit Nashville

<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=122 printed_page=120 -->

**MINORITY REPORT - OVERTURE 28**

The minority moves the following as a substitution motion:

**Therefore be it resolved** that the 2019 General Assembly of the

Presbyterian Church in America adopts the following 12 articles to
affirm the Bible’s teaching about homosexuality.

**Scripture Proofs and Reasoning**

Whereas a controversy has arisen in the Presbyterian Church in America over
certain teachings espoused by Revoice, an organization that is promoting
LGBT+ flourishing in various PCA churches, it has become necessary to
clarify and reaffirm what the Bible teaches concerning homosexuality and
gender.

**Article 1**

WE AFFIRM that homosexual attraction, homosexual orientation, and
homosexual acts are against nature and sinful.

Romans 1:26-27 For this reason God _gave them up to vile passions_ . For even
their _women exchanged the natural use for what is against nature. 27_
_Likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their_
_lust for one another,_ men with men committing what is shameful, and
receiving in themselves the penalty of their error which was due.

**The Apostle says that the vile passions that are behind homosexual sin**
**are against nature. They are against God’s natural created order.**

WE DENY that homosexual attraction is part of the original creation order,
since God created man in His image male and female for one another.

**Attraction to the opposite sex is according to God’s created order. God**
**has made men and women for one another with the intention that they**
Genesis 2:18 And the LORD God said, "It is not good that man should be
alone; _I will make him a helper comparable to him."_

Genesis 2:21-24 And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall on Adam,
and he slept; and He took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh in its place.
22 Then the rib which the LORD God had taken from man He made into a

woman, and He brought her to the man. 23 And Adam said: "This is now
bone of my bones And flesh of my flesh; She shall be called Woman,
Because she was taken out of Man." 24 _Therefore a man shall leave his father_
_and mother and be joined to his wife, and they shall become one flesh._

Genesis 1:26-28 Then God said, "Let Us make man in Our image, according
to Our likeness; let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, over the

<a id="ga47-p121"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=123 printed_page=121 -->

birds of the air, and over the cattle, over all the earth and over every creeping
thing that creeps on the earth." 27 _So God created man in His own image; in_
_the image of God He created him; male and female He created them_ . 28 Then
God blessed them, and God said to them, " _Be fruitful and multiply; fill the_
_earth and subdue it;_ have dominion over the fish of the sea, over the birds of
the air, and over every living thing that moves on the earth."

Jesus confirmed God’s original purpose for man and woman.

Matthew 19:4-6 And He answered and said to them, "Have you not read that
He who made them at the beginning 'MADE THEM MALE AND
FEMALE,' 5 and said, 'FOR THIS REASON A MAN SHALL LEAVE HIS
FATHER AND MOTHER AND BE JOINED TO HIS WIFE, AND THE
TWO SHALL BECOME ONE FLESH' ? 6 So then, they are no longer two
but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let not man separate."

**Article 2**
WE AFFIRM that homosexual attraction is a vile, degrading, and
dishonorable passion, and those who condone homosexuality invite the
further judgment of God.

BGT Romans 1:26 Διὰ τοῦτο παρέδωκεν αὐτοὺς ὁ θεὸς εἰς _**πάθη ἀτιμίας**_, αἵ
τε γὰρ θήλειαι αὐτῶν μετήλλαξαν τὴν φυσικὴν χρῆσιν εἰς τὴν παρὰ φύσιν,

NKJV Romans 1:26 For this reason God gave them up to _vile passions_ . For
even their women exchanged the natural use for what is against nature.

NAU Romans 1:26 For this reason God gave them over to _degrading_
_passions_ ; for their women exchanged the natural function for that which is
unnatural,

ESV Romans 1:26 For this reason God gave them up to _dishonorable_
_passions_ . For their women exchanged natural relations for those that are
contrary to nature;

Paul says that unnatural sexual attractions for the same sex are πάθη ἀτιμίας.
**This is translated in three different ways: vile passions, degrading**
**passions, and dishonorable passions. The affirmation gives the full**
**meaning of the word ἀτιμίας.**

American Psychological Association defines Sexual Orientation:

_Sexual orientation refers to an enduring pattern of emotional, romantic_
_and/or sexual attractions to men, women or both sexes_ . Sexual orientation
also refers to a person's sense of identity based on those attractions, related
behaviors and membership in a community of others who share those
attractions…Sexual orientation is usually discussed in terms of three

<a id="ga47-p122"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=124 printed_page=122 -->

categories: heterosexual (having emotional, romantic or sexual attractions to
members of the other sex), _gay/lesbian (having emotional, romantic or_
_sexual attractions to members of one's own sex)_ and bisexual (having
emotional, romantic or sexual attractions to both men and women).

**If homosexual attraction is sinful, and homosexual orientation is an**
**enduring pattern of emotional, romantic, and sexual attraction to the**
**same sex, then both the attraction and orientation are sinful. The**
**incidents of homosexual attraction are sins and the homosexual**
**orientation is a pattern of sinful attractions.**

Romans 1:26-32 For this reason _God gave them up to vile passions_ . For even
their women exchanged the natural use for what is against nature. 27
Likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their
lust for one another, men with men committing what is shameful, and
receiving in themselves the penalty of their error which was due. 28 And
even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, _God gave them_

unloving, unforgiving, unmerciful; 32 who, _knowing the righteous judgment_
_of God, that those who practice such things are deserving of death, not only_
_do the same but also approve of those who practice them._

Romans 1:26-32 teaches that **homosexual sin is a consequence of God**
**giving sinners over to vile passions (v.26) and a debased mind (v.28).** This
debased mind is the origin of all sorts of unfitting things (vv.29-31). These
sins deserve the righteous judgment of God and deserve death (v.32). **Thus**
**those who practice these sins and approve of this sin invite the further**
**judgment of God.**

WE DENY that unrepentant homosexuals will inherit the kingdom of God.

**Article 4**

WE AFFIRM that those with homosexual attractions, incestuous attractions,
and bestial attractions are ‘sexual minorities’ in the church.

**Teachers connected with Revoice regularly referred to same-sex**
**attracted people as ‘sexual minorities.’** This is accurate in the sense that
those who struggle with same-sex attraction are a minority in number.

Christians particularly want to champion oppressed minorities, **but being a**
**minority is not in itself virtuous.** Those with incestuous, bestial, or
pedophilic orientations are also in the minority, but we should not be fooled
into sympathy with sexual immorality because they are minorities.

**Furthermore, LGBT+ identities are not analogous to racial identities.**
**Race is immutable and has nothing to do with unlawful attractions or**
**desires. LGBT+ identities are mutable and center around unlawful**
**attractions and desires. Race is benign, unlawful sexual attraction is sin.**

**Many speakers at the Revoice conference were playing the part of an**
**oppressed minority, but looking around at the larger culture,**
**homosexuals are a powerful force to be reckoned with** . It is bakers and
photographers who dare to stand against their political agenda who are being
sued and losing their businesses. **The homosexuals are not an oppressed**
**minority, but a persecuting minority.**

**The faithful church is not persecuting homosexuals, but the faithful**
**church merely continues to disapprove of the sin of homosexuality from**
**its origin in the heart to its manifestation in effeminacy, gender**
**confusion, and physical sex** . The church is right in denying those who

<a id="ga47-p124"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=126 printed_page=124 -->

identify as homosexuals to serve in the church. Homosexuals should be loved
and ministered to, but they cannot join the church until they repent of their
sin.

WE DENY that there is any virtue in being identified as a ‘sexual minority,’
because all of these dispositions are sinful.

Leviticus 18:6 'None of you shall approach anyone who is near of kin to him,
to uncover his nakedness: I am the LORD.

Leviticus 18:22-23 you shall not lie with a male as with a woman. It is an

abomination. 23 Nor shall you mate with any animal, to defile yourself with
it. Nor shall any woman stand before an animal to mate with it. It is
perversion.

Leviticus 20:13 if a man lies with a male as he lies with a woman, both of
them have committed an abomination. They shall surely be put to death.
Their blood shall be upon them.

Leviticus 20:15-16 If a man mates with an animal, he shall surely be put to
death, and you shall kill the animal. 16 If a woman approaches any animal
and mates with it, you shall kill the woman and the animal. They shall surely
be put to death. Their blood is upon them.

**There is no virtue in sexual minority status, especially when it is sexual**
**sin that puts you in the minority.**

**Article 6**

WE AFFIRM that many people having a homosexual orientation do not

**concluded that this is how God made them. This is an invalid deduction.**

WE DENY that unwanted sexual attractions and orientations are natural,
neutral, or good.

Romans 7:22-23 For I delight in the law of God according to the inward man.
23 But I see another law in my members, warring against the law of my mind,
and bringing me into captivity to the law of sin which is in my members.

**Those who delight in the law of God have a sinful nature that is warring**
**against the new nature. They not only have unwanted desires but do**
**things they do not want to do at some level** . The presence of unwanted
attractions is part of the Christian experience, but it is wrong to deduce that
these affections and inclinations are natural, neutral, or good, when God
condemns them.

Galatians 5:16-17 I say then: Walk in the Spirit, and you shall not fulfill the
lust of the flesh. 17 _For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit_
_against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, so that you do not_
_do the things that you wish._

**No doubt this war between the flesh and the spirit makes us wretched,**

body of death? 25 I thank God--through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then, with

WE AFFIRM that Christians ought to abhor homosexual sin. Abhorrence of

Leviticus 18:22 you shall not lie with a male as with a woman. It is an

Psalm 97:10 You who love the LORD, hate evil!

**God calls us to hate all sin, particularly sexual sin. We should abhor all**

WE DENY that revulsion and abhorrence of homosexual sin is sinful.

Romans 12:9 Let love be without hypocrisy. _Abhor what is evil_ . Cling to
what is good.

**Christians are not called to overlook evil or redefine it. The one who**

1 Corinthians 13:4-7 Love suffers long and is kind; love does not envy; love
does not parade itself, is not puffed up; 5 does not behave rudely, does not
seek its own, is not provoked, thinks no evil; 6 _does not rejoice in iniquity,_
_but rejoices in the truth_ ; 7 bears all things, believes all things, hopes all
things, endures all things.

**Those who rejoice in and celebrate ‘homosexual love’ are rejoicing in**

abhorring their sin. Love is patient and kind and does not behave rudely.
Love does not seek its selfish interests but rejoices in the truth, including the
truth that God hates sexual sin.

WE AFFIRM that people living in homosexual sin should be shown kindness

WE DENY that it is loving or kind to try to diminish or remove the shame

**Homosexual attractions and the acts they desire are shameful.**

Romans 1:26-27 For this reason God gave them up to vile passions. For even
their women exchanged the natural use for what is against nature. 27
Likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their
lust for one another, men with men _committing what is shameful_, and
receiving in themselves the penalty of their error which was due.

**It is right to blush and be ashamed of abominations. Shame for sin is a**
**means of grace that leads us to repentance. It is right to have deep and**

Jeremiah 6:14-15 They have also healed the hurt of My people slightly,
Saying, 'Peace, peace!' When there is no peace. 15 _Were they ashamed when_

Philippians 3:18-19 For many walk, of whom I have told you often, and now
tell you even weeping, that they are the enemies of the cross of Christ: 19
whose end is destruction, whose god is their belly, and _whose glory is in their_

<a id="ga47-p128"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=130 printed_page=128 -->

**The faithful friend rebukes sin and encourages blushing for sin. Sinners**
**should be ashamed of their sins which lead to death and submit to God**

Romans 6:21-23 _What fruit did you have then in the things of which you are_
_now ashamed? For the end of those things is death_ . 22 But now having been
set free from sin, and having become slaves of God, you have your fruit to
holiness, and the end, everlasting life. 23 For the wages of sin is death, but
the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

**When God gives a sinner the ability to blush for their sin and fear the**
**death it brings, this is grace. In Christ, we are set free from sin and made**
**slaves to God who alone gives us eternal life. To coddle sin is death. To**
**forsake it and cling to Christ is eternal life.**

if a man has a deadly disease and you have the cure, then you must tell him
of his disease and offer him the cure. You must persuade him of the deadly
nature of his disease. it would be wicked to say, “Your fine, your fine,” when
he is dying. The first step to escaping abominable sin is to acknowledge that
it is an abomination. It is right to be ashamed of sin and blush for it. It is good
to turn from it to Christ who alone can take away the shame of our sin.

**Article 9**

WE AFFIRM that repentant sinners turning from all unnatural attractions
and trusting in Christ should be welcomed into the church as members in
good standing.

1 Timothy 1:15-16 This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptance, that
Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom i am chief. 16
However, for this reason I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might
show all longsuffering, as a pattern to those who are going to believe on Him
for everlasting life.

**Christ saves sinners. Some people are greater sinners than others, but**
**God saved the chief sinner—the Apostle Paul—as a pattern to those who**
**are going to believe on Him for everlasting life. Thus, repentant sinners**
**turning from abominable sins to trust in Christ Jesus, the Savior of**
**sinners, should be welcomed into the church with open arms.**

WE DENY that followers of Christ who struggle with homosexual attraction
should be identified as “LGBT+ Christians,” because it dishonors Christ to
identify with sinful attractions and orientations.

1 Corinthians 6:9-11 Or do you not know that the unrighteous will not inherit
the kingdom of God? Do not be deceived; neither fornicators, nor idolaters,

<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=131 printed_page=129 -->

nor adulterers, nor effeminate, nor homosexuals, 10 nor thieves, nor the
covetous, nor drunkards, nor revilers, nor swindlers, will inherit the kingdom

_sanctified, but you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in_
_the Spirit of our God. (NASB)_

**If a person practices fornication, idolatry, adultery, homosexuality, or**
**drunkenness, then they are rightly labeled fornicators, idolaters,**
**adulterers, homosexuals, or drunkards. If, however, they forsake these**
**sins and turn to the Lord Jesus Christ, and by the power of His Spirit**

**They are washed, sanctified, and justified in the name of the Lord Jesus**
**Christ and in the Spirit of our God. To label them with the degrading**

2 Corinthians 5:16-17 Therefore, from now on, we regard no one according
to the flesh. Even though we have known Christ according to the flesh, yet
now we know Him thus no longer. 17 Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is

new.

Romans 6:21-23 _What fruit did you have then in the things of which you are_

set free from sin, and having become slaves of God, you have your fruit to
holiness, and the end, everlasting life. 23 For the wages of sin is death, but
the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

**I fear that some who identify as ‘Gay Christians’ do not understand the**
**shame of their sin. They still want to glory in what is shameful. The**

**Those who understand the shame of their idolatry, adultery,**
**drunkenness, and fornication do not want to identify as “Idolatrous**

**man who still struggles with lust for women other than his wife does not**
**want to identify a san “Adulterous Christian.” Why is homosexuality a**
**special sin that deserves recognition? We fear it may be the first steps**

**“The Overhauling of Straight America” by Marshall Kirk and Erastes**

WE AFFIRM that homosexual attraction should be mortified by the power
of the Spirit.

Colossians 3:5-7 _Therefore put to death your members which are on the_
_earth: fornication, uncleanness, passion, evil desire, and covetousness,_

_the sons of disobedience, 7 in which you yourselves once walked when you_
_lived in them._

Romans 8:12-14 Therefore, brethren, we are debtors--not to the flesh, to live
according to the flesh. 13 For if you live according to the flesh you will die;
but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live. 14
For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.

**All sexual immorality in thought, word, or deed is to be avoided. We are**
