**women in the PCA.**

Rationale: After Jesus rose from the dead, He commanded His apostles:
“Therefore, go make disciples of all nations” (Matt. 28:18). Jesus, our

<a id="ga47-p698"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=700 printed_page=698 -->

great King, prescribes the mission of His church, which, in part, is to
ensure the nations become His disciples (cf. WCF 7.6). The overflow of
the Great Commission is revealed in Acts. The names in Acts 13:1
demonstrate that Jesus’ disciples took His commission seriously. Indeed,
people from different ethnic backgrounds were coming to faith in Christ.
Unfortunately, the PCA, though it upholds the mandate to make disciples
of the nations, has yet to see the demographics in diverse communities
reflected in Mission to the World. Therefore, as the MTW considers the
Great Commission afresh, we should affirm that intentionally reaching
the nations in their communities is not an addition to the church’s

ministry. It is a demand, even command, by Jesus as a principal part of
their church’s ministry. Acts 6:1-7 sets precedent for affirming the
underprivileged. Whether male or female, everyone deserved equal
treatment in Christ’s church (cf. Jas. 2:1-13).

**Application of Recommendations:**

Each of these recommendations made by the Report and adopted by the 45 [th]
General Assembly applies to Mission to the World, in that each of
recommendation is made to PCA presbyteries and to General Assembly.
Mission to the World, as a committee of the General Assembly, is thereby
encouraged to adopt these recommendations. We believe these
recommendations are commended by CMTW to every member of MTW, as
summed up in the Statement: “Therefore, MTW is committed to supporting
and equipping our sisters in Christ as they exercise their gifts and abilities
throughout MTW's global ministry as together we show and tell the gospel
of God's grace.”

Specifically, these recommendations encourage Mission to the World to:

1. Recognize various views and practices in the PCA within “scriptural

and constitutional parameters,” and to respect and support those
various views and practices;
2. Strive to develop, recognize, and utilize the gifts, skills, knowledge,

and wisdom of godly women, and promoting and recruiting them to

serve.

3. Consider how to affirm and include underprivileged and

underrepresented women in Mission to the World.

The CMTW-adopted Statement on Valuing Women seeks to encourage
everyone in Mission to the World to consider and embrace these
recommendations from the General Assembly. There are various ways field
teams and leadership may go about following these recommendations.

<a id="ga47-p699"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=701 printed_page=699 -->

For example: spending a season in prayer about how to implement
these recommendations; inviting women to attend leadership development
events in order to identify and increase their skills and gifts; including the
voices of women in brainstorming, vision casting, or other future-oriented
planning sessions; conducting a “gifts survey” of a field team, including all
members of the team; asking with intentionality if there might be a woman
who could serve in open or new leadership positions. Any of these and
more would be helpful starting points that lead to intentionally looking for
opportunities to deploy gifts not currently being used.

**Considerations for MTW Field Operations:**

The concluding paragraph in the CMTW-adopted Statement on Valuing
Women may be a challenging portion for regions and teams to implement,
and may lead to questions of application. That portion of the statement reads:
“In keeping with this statement, we affirm that women may serve in
leadership roles within MTW, recognizing that only ordained elders exercise
ecclesiastical authority over church plants or churches.” In order to assist
teams and regions in making decisions, this list of considerations is provided
as a guide to assist in thinking through specific application.

First, however, is what is meant by “ecclesiastical authority over church
plants or churches?” Helpful here are two paragraphs from the PCA _Book of_
_Church Order_, chapter 3:

**3-2.** Ecclesiastical power, which is wholly spiritual, is twofold. The
officers exercise it sometimes severally, as in preaching the Gospel,
administering the Sacraments, reproving the erring, visiting the
sick, and comforting the afflicted, which is the power of order; and
they exercise it sometimes jointly in Church courts, after the form
of judgment, which is the power of jurisdiction.

**3-6.** The exercise of ecclesiastical power, whether joint or several,
has the divine sanction when in conformity with the statutes
enacted by Christ, the Lawgiver, and when put forth by courts or
by officers appointed thereunto in His Word.

Ecclesiastical power, as defined by the _BCO_, means that power that is
exercised by a church officer when serving in the role of his office, or by a
Church court, such as a Session, Presbytery, or General Assembly.
Therefore, this portion of the statement indicates a woman can serve in
leadership, other than in roles that would exercise (ecclesiastical) authority
over church plants or churches.

<a id="ga47-p700"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=702 printed_page=700 -->

With this understanding, here is a list of considerations that could help in
thinking through MTW leadership appointments and clarifying when the
person in those positions would exercise ecclesiastical authority over church
or church plants:

1. if a leader has no direct authority over ordained elders serving ina

church or church planting context, appointment of a woman as leader
should be considered.
2. Would a leader have direct authority over an elder in the elder’s exercise

of his ecclesiastical responsibilities? If so, then an ordained elder is

necessary.
3. Would a leader have direct leadership authority for one or more ordained

elders serving ina church orin a church planting context? If so, then:
a. Would that leader have leadership responsibilities apart from those

responsibilities which are ordinarily the responsibility of ordained
elders or church courts? AND

b. Is there another ordained elder in the region in line authority to

whom church matters (apart from applicable church court) can be
deferred, in consultation with the leader?
If so, appointment of a woman or other non-ordained leader is possible
in that leadership role.
4. Does a specific national context or partnership only welcome ordained

elders as partners in leadership roles? We commend discussion with
national partners and local leaders when considering these appointments
so that the local context is well understood. We propose there are situations
in which the degree of the exclusion of women or non-ordained men
from participation in a culture must be challenged in healthy ways. At
the same time, there may be situations in which teaching and biblical
education of partners might need to precede appointment of nonordained leaders in contexts that are culturally (but not biblically)
exclusive, so as not to offend national partners in such contexts. However
a biblical “critical contextualization [19] ” should encourage us, and our
partners, in a direction that embraces men and women serving in
appropriate roles in the church when the leadership roles are not
exercising ecclesiastical authority over churches or church plants.
**Conclusion**
Our desire is to see our missionary community value our women colleagues

19 Dr. Paul Heibert, Critical Contextualization. International Bulletin 11:3:104-112. July.

Reprinted in The Best in Theology: Volume Two, J. 1. Packer, General Editor. Wheaton:
Christianity Today. pp. 387-400.

<a id="ga47-p701"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=703 printed_page=701 -->

throughout our organization, seeking to deploy the gifts that God has given
His Church through every one of our members. At the same time, we seek to
affirm and uphold the ecclesiastical authority of the Church through qualified
ordained men. MTW, the Committee on Mission to the World, and the 44 [th]
General Assembly of the PCA recognize we can, and must, seek to further
encourage and challenge ourselves to pursue these recommendations, that the
Church may be strengthened with all of the gifts of the Giver, for His glory.

##### OVERTURE 42 from Chicago Metro Presbytery (to AC, OC)

“Establish Study Committee on Sexuality”

**Whereas**, in recent years a dramatic shift in cultural norms and attitudes

away from Biblical teaching and morality concerning human sexuality
has taken place; and

**Whereas**, the current cultural climate presents an apologetic and

evangelistic challenge to the Church as she seeks to articulate the
Biblical teaching on human sexuality clearly and effectively to those
outside; and

**Whereas**, this cultural climate also fosters increasingly complex pastoral

situations as the Church seeks to minister to those who struggle with
issues related to their own sexuality; and

**Whereas**, some of the flock in our midst are among those hard-pressed

with temptation that manifests itself through same sex-attraction or a
confused sexual identity, and many sessions may feel overwhelmed or
underequipped to minister to them; and

**Whereas**, though the PCA issued statements in 1977, 1996, and 1999 on the

Biblical view concerning homosexuality, these statements were
delivered before the more recent cultural changes with their attendant
challenges, and the PCA has yet to undertake an extensive study of the
topic; and

**Whereas**, the PCA has the Biblical, theological, and confessional resources

to apply the unchanging gospel to the issues surrounding human
sexuality in our current cultural environment;

**Therefore, be it resolved** that the 47 [th ] General Assembly create an Ad
Interim Committee (AIC) to study the topic of human sexuality with
particular attention to the issues of homosexuality, same- sex attraction,
and transgenderism, and prepare a report which:

<a id="ga47-p702"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=704 printed_page=702 -->

- Shall include an annotated bibliography of resources on sexuality
which the Committee endorses to help pastors and sessions shepherd
congregants who are dealing with same-sex attraction; and

- Shall address: (1) the nature of temptation, sin, and repentance as
regards same sex- attraction; (2) the propriety of using terms like
“gay Christian” when referring to a believer struggling with samesex attraction; (3) the status of "orientation" as a valid
anthropological category; (4) the practice of “spiritual friendship”
among same sex attracted Christians; and

- Shall include suggested ways to articulate and defend a Biblical
understanding of homosexuality, same sex-attraction, and
transgenderism in the context of a culture that denies that
understanding; and

- Should the Committee conclude it prudent, may, as one of its
recommendations, propose a statement on the topic; and

**Be it further resolved,** that, in its examination of [20] Scripture and the PCA

_Confession of Faith_ and Catechisms, the Committee consider studies
and statements of other bodies outside and within the PCA (including,
but not limited to, the Council on Biblical Manhood and Womanhood’s
“Nashville Statement”; the RPCNA’s “Contemporary Perspectives on
Sexual Orientation: A Theological and Pastoral Analysis”; the RPCES’
“Pastoral Care for the Repentant Homosexual”; Missouri Presbytery’s
“Homosexuality and the Gospel of Grace: Faithfulness to the Lord’s
Calling in an Age of Sexual Autonomy”; the statements on
homosexuality issued by the 5 [th], 24 [th], and 27 [th ] General Assemblies of
the PCA and the January 14, 2015, Report of the CMC Subcommittee
on Homosexuality) and resources recommended in those documents; and

**Be it further resolved** that the Moderator of the 47 [th ] General Assembly

appoint the seven voting members who shall be either PCA teaching or
PCA ruling elders, and the Committee shall include at least two teaching
and two ruling elders including members whose writings reflect
theological engagement on the issue and pastoral interaction with those
dealing with same sex attraction and transgenderism; and

**Be it further resolved** that the Committee be permitted to recruit others to

serve the Committee as advisors within the confines of its budget, with

20 The Ohio presbytery version here reads “that in addition to Scripture and the PCA

_Confession of Faith_ and Catechisms….” Trinity’s Session made the small amendment to
“in its examination of…”

<a id="ga47-p703"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=705 printed_page=703 -->

particular concern to secure advisors with experience in counseling those
dealing with same-sex attraction and transgenderism; and

**Be it further resolved** that the budget for the study committee be set at

$15,000/year and that funds be derived from gifts to the AC
designated for that purpose; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall present its report to the

48 [th ] General Assembly, including any recommendations it may
present.

_Adopted by Chicago Metro Presbytery at its stated meeting, May 15, 2019_
_Attested by /s/ TE Kyle Edwards, stated clerk_

##### OVERTURE 43 from Metro Atlanta Presbytery (to AC, OC)

“Formation of a Study Committee on Domestic Abuse and
Sexual Assault” (Version 8)

**Whereas**, the sins of domestic abuse, including physical, emotional, and

verbal abuse, and sexual assault are expressly forbidden in scripture
(Deuteronomy 22:25-27; Eph. 5:25-29; 2 Timothy 3:2-5; 1 Peter 3:7;
Jude 7; Exodus 21:15; also WLC 135 and 139); and

**Whereas**, these sins are especially heinous because they are “against the

express letter of the law,” “break many commandments,” “admit of no
reparation,” and often involve various other aggravations (WLC 151);
and

**Whereas**, these sins are grievously prevalent in our society as well as the

Church; and

**Whereas**, these sins cause a complex set of physical, emotional, spiritual,

and psychological responses—including immense shame, fear and
anxiety, depression, trauma, isolation, anger, and denial—that persist
with varying levels of severity throughout life; and since these heinous
sins do lasting damage to every aspect of a victims life (e.g., 2 Samuel
13:1-22); and

**Whereas**, it is the responsibility of the elders of the church to exercise their

authority, severally and jointly, to shepherd those who are under their
care by guarding against such sinful behavior as much as possible, by
wisely caring for the vulnerable and those victimized by the sins of
others, and by appropriately disciplining those guilty of such sins
toward a full repentance in each case in accordance with applicable law
(Ezekiel 34:1-24; James 1:27; BCO 8-3; 27-4); and

**Whereas**, these sins have been a concerning subject of attention not only in

other denominations (cf. the Roman Catholic Church and the Southern
Baptist Convention) but even in the proceedings of the 46th General
Assembly of the PCA; and

<a id="ga47-p704"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=706 printed_page=704 -->

**Whereas**, the 42nd General Assembly previously exhorted the PCA and its

courts to respond boldly against one form of these sins, namely the
sexual abuse of children; and

**Whereas**, the PCA report on “Divorce and Remarriage” was limited in its

focus and does not adequately define the scope and nature of domestic
abuse and sexual assault nor adequately address many pastoral concerns
regarding the care of those victimized by such sins; and

**Whereas**, no standardized resources for pastoral care on these sins and their

complex consequences exist for officers, church courts, and agencies in
the PCA; and

**Whereas**, no clear guidelines for policy or practice with regard to these sins

exist for the officers, church courts, and agencies in the PCA that would
protect individuals and congregations; and

**Whereas**, the Rules of Discipline protect against unjust accusation ( _BCO_ 31
8), they also make no consideration for the unique heinousness of these
sins and their practical effect on victims;

**Therefore, be it resolved** that Metro Atlanta Presbytery hereby overtures

the 47th General Assembly of the PCA to form a committee to study:
(1) the best practices and policy guidelines for elders, church courts, and

agencies for justly and lawfully protecting against these sins, for
providing safety for potential victims who come forward, and for
responding to those guilty of these sins; and
(2) the possibility of changes to the Rules of Discipline that would

appropriately protect victims of these sins within a biblical process;
and

(3) the pastoral resources available for the care of survivors of these sins

as well as ministry and counsel for those guilty of such sins;
(4) the possible need for repentance as a church; and
(5) to include in their report a definition of the scope and nature of

domestic abuse and sexual assault; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall be appointed by the

moderator, and shall include among its members or advisory members at
least two subject-matter experts and at least two women; and

**Be it further resolved** that the budget for the study committee be set at

$15,000/year and that funds be derived from gifts to the AC designated for
that purpose, with **Metro Atlanta** Presbytery committed to offer $ **1,000** ; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall present their findings and

recommendations for consideration by the 48th General Assembly.

_Adopted by Metro Atlanta Presbytery at its stated meeting, May 7, 2019_
_Attested by /s/ TE Randy Schlichting, stated clerk_

<a id="ga47-p705"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=707 printed_page=705 -->

##### OVERTURE 44 from Ohio Valley Presbytery (to AC, OC)

Establish Ad Interim Committee to Study Human Sexuality

**Whereas**, in recent years a dramatic shift in cultural norms and attitudes

away from Biblical teaching and morality concerning human sexuality
has taken place; and

**Whereas**, the current cultural climate presents an apologetic and evangelistic

challenge to the Church as she seeks to articulate the Biblical teaching
on human sexuality clearly and effectively to those outside; and

**Whereas**, this cultural climate also fosters increasingly complex pastoral

situations as the Church seeks to minister to those who struggle with
issues related to their own sexuality; and

**Whereas**, some of the flock in our midst are among those hard-pressed with

temptation that manifests itself through same sex-attraction or a confused
sexual identity, and many sessions may feel overwhelmed or
underequipped to minister to them; and

**Whereas**, though the PCA issued statements in 1977, 1996, and 1999 on the

Biblical view concerning homosexuality, these statements were
delivered before the more recent cultural changes with their attendant
challenges, and the PCA has yet to undertake an extensive study of the
topic; and

**Whereas**, the PCA has the Biblical, theological and confessional resources

to apply the unchanging gospel to the issues surrounding human
sexuality in our current cultural environment,

**Therefore, be it resolved** that the 47 [th] General Assembly create an Ad
Interim Committee (AIC) to study the topic of human sexuality with
particular attention to the issues of homosexuality, same-sex attraction,
transgenderism, and gender dysphoria, and prepare a report which:

- Shall include an annotated bibliography of resources on sexuality
which the Committee endorses to help pastors and sessions shepherd
congregants who are dealing with same-sex attraction; and

- Shall address: (1) the nature of temptation, sin, and repentance as
regards same sex-attraction; (2) the propriety of using terms like
“gay Christian” when referring to a believer struggling with samesex attraction; (3) The status of "orientation" as a valid
anthropological category; (4) the practice of “spiritual friendship”
among same sex attracted Christians; and

<a id="ga47-p706"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=708 printed_page=706 -->

- Shall include suggested ways to articulate and defend a Biblical
understanding of homosexuality, same sex-attraction, transgenderism,
and gender dysphoria in the context of a culture that denies that
understanding; and

- Should the Committee conclude it prudent, may, as one of its
recommendations, propose a statement on the topic; and

**Be it further resolved,** that, in addition to Scripture and the PCA Confession

of Faith and Catechisms, the Committee consider studies and statements
of other bodies outside and within the PCA (including, but not limited
to, the Council on Biblical Manhood and Womanhood’s “Nashville
Statement”; the RPCNA’s “Contemporary Perspectives on Sexual
Orientation: A Theological and Pastoral Analysis”; the RPCES’
“Pastoral Care for the Repentant Homosexual”; Missouri Presbytery’s
“Homosexuality and the Gospel of Grace: Faithfulness to the Lord’s
Calling in an Age of Sexual Autonomy”; the statements on
homosexuality issued by the 5 [th], 24 [th], and 27 [th] General Assemblies of the
PCA and the January 14, 2015, Report of the CMC Subcommittee on
Homosexuality) and resources recommended in those documents; and

**Be it further resolved** that the Moderator of the 47 [th] General Assembly

appoint the seven voting members who shall be either PCA teaching or
PCA ruling elders, and the Committee shall include at least two teaching
and two ruling elders including members whose writings reflect
theological engagement on the issue and pastoral interaction with those
dealing with same sex attraction, transgenderism, and gender dysphoria;
and

**Be it further resolved** that the Committee be permitted to recruit others to

serve the Committee as advisors within the confines of its budget, with
particular concern to secure advisors with experience in counseling those
dealing with same-sex attraction, transgenderism, and gender dysphoria;
and

**Be it further resolved** that the budget for the study committee be set at

$15,000/year and that funds be derived from gifts to the AC designated
for that purpose; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall present its report to the 48 [th]

General Assembly, including any recommendations it may present.

_Adopted by Ohio Valley Presbytery at its stated meeting, May 21, 2019_
_Attested by /s/ TE Larry C. Hoop, stated clerk._

<a id="ga47-p707"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=709 printed_page=707 -->

##### OVERTURE 45 from Missouri Presbytery (to AC, OC)

“Form an Ad Interim Committee to Seek Consensus on

Doctrinal Boundaries and Pastoral Care in the Current
Debates About Sexuality”

**Whereas** there is great responsibility and opportunity, occasioned by the

cultural moment, for the church to be better equipped to announce and
embody the gracious, dignifying welcome and life-giving wisdom found
in the Gospel of Jesus Christ to non-Christians who live with
homosexuality or experience unique manifestations of brokenness in
matters of gender and sexuality; and

**Whereas** there is significant complexity to some important aspects of the

theological, pastoral, terminological, and missiological issues
underlying homosexuality; and

**Whereas** there is already significant disagreement and discord within the

PCA with respect to:

- approaches to pastoral care for Christians living with homosexuality;
and

- the use of such terms as “sexual orientation,” “gay,” “sexual
minorities,” “gay Christian” and the “identity questions” it raises,
etc., and whether such use constitutes profound doctrinal or perhaps
practical error, and ought to be soundly condemned and censured or,
on the other hand, tolerated, with proper caveats and qualifications; and

**Whereas** doctrinal boundaries and pastoral applications of sensitive and/or

controversial issues are best made by deliberative and collaborative
debate, study, conversation, and consensus-building rather than by
judicial fiat in investigations and trials, a principle that was demonstrated
to be so helpful in the case of the Ad-Interim Committee on Divorce and
Remarriage (1992) and the Ad-interim Committee on the Days of
Creation (2000); and

**Whereas** the peace and purity of the church as well as _the integrity of and_

_energy for the mission of the church to our secular culture_ is at stake in
these current debates; and

**Whereas** our Presbytery _ad hoc_ committee appointed to investigate

Memorial PCA’s hosting of the 2018 Revoice conference, after giving
careful consideration to the underlying theological, pastoral, and
missiological matters, recommended that we overture the General
Assembly for a further in-depth study/consensus-building committee for
the purpose of coming to greater doctrinal clarity in pursuit of a deeper
church unity on issues touching homosexuality;

<a id="ga47-p708"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=710 printed_page=708 -->

**Therefore, be it resolved** that the 47th General Assembly create an Ad

Interim Committee (AIC) to study the topic of sexuality with particular
attention to the issue of where the church has failed in its ministry to
same-sex-attracted men and women, and what the church ought to be
doing now in its ministry to them, while neither compromising the gospel
nor dulling the vitality of its message to lost people.

The committee is tasked with preparing a report which:

- Shall seek, as much as consciences permit, _to build a consensus on_
_these difficult matters_ so as to secure a place, within trusted
boundaries, for our wider church to grow in their witness to Christ
and live at peace with one another; and

- Shall address: (1) the nature of temptation, sin, and repentance as
regards same-sex-attraction; (2) the propriety of using terms like
“gay,” “gay Christian” when referring to a believer living with samesex attraction; (3) The status of "orientation" as a valid
anthropological category; (4) the practice of “celibate partnerships”
and “romantic coupling” for same-sex-attracted Christians; and
`o` Shall include suggested ways to articulate and defend a Biblical

understanding of homosexuality in the context of a culture that
denies that understanding; and
`o` Shall include an annotated bibliography of resources on

sexuality which the Committee recommends for helping pastors
and Sessions shepherd their people who live with same-sex
attraction; and
`o` This Committee may:

a. report to the 48th GA in Birmingham (2020) any statement

it adopts as a Committee, without needing to move it for GA
adoption.
b. recommend to the 48th GA any statement it believes would

be prudent and warranted for the Assembly to adopt.

**Be it further resolved** that the Moderator of the 47th General Assembly

appoint the seven voting members who shall be either PCA teaching or
PCA ruling elders (at least two of each kind), including members whose
writings have included theological engagement on the issue and
members who have had pastoral interaction with those struggling with
same-sex attraction; and

**Be it further resolved** that the Committee be permitted to recruit others,

within the confines of its budget, to serve as its advisors, regardless of
denomination or gender, with particular concern to secure those with
experience in counseling those dealing with same-sex attraction; and

<a id="ga47-p709"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=711 printed_page=709 -->

**Be it further resolved,** that, in addition to Scripture and the PCA _Confession_

_of Faith_ and Catechisms, the Committee consider studies and statements
of other bodies outside and within the PCA (including, but not limited
to, the Council on Biblical Manhood and Womanhood’s “Nashville
Statement”; the RPCNA’s “Contemporary Perspectives on Sexual
Orientation: A Theological and Pastoral Analysis”; the RPCES’
“Pastoral Care for the Repentant Homosexual”; Missouri Presbytery’s
“Homosexuality and the Gospel of Grace: Faithfulness to the Lord’s
Calling in an Age of Sexual Autonomy” and “Missouri Presbytery Ad
Hoc Committee to Investigate Memorial Presbyterian Church for
Hosting the Revoice 18 Conference in July 2018”; the statements on
homosexuality issued by the 5th, 24th, and 27th General Assemblies of
the PCA and the January 14, 2015, Report of the CMC Subcommittee on
Homosexuality) and resources recommended in those documents; and

**Be it further resolved** that the budget for the study committee be set at

$15,000/year and that funds be derived from gifts to the AC designated
for that purpose; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall present its report to the 48th

General Assembly, meeting in Birmingham, Alabama in 2020; and

**Be it further resolved** that the 47th GA authorize the GA Moderator to

appoint the seven voting members of this Committee, **the slate to be**
**approved by the vote of the GA before dismissal of the 47** **[th]** **GA**, per
_RAO_ 9-4 and _RAO_ 19-1 (Robert's Rules, RONR, 11th ed., pp. 174-175,
pp. 495-496, and p. 579).

_Adopted by Missouri Presbytery at its called meeting on May 18, 2019._
_Attested by /s/ TE Timothy R. Butler, stated clerk_

##### OVERTURE 46 from Hills and Plains Presbytery (OC)

“Reaffirm PCA’s Commitment Regarding the Sanctity of Life and Our
Stance Against Abortion in Particular”

**Whereas**, the Word of God indicates that all human beings are created in the

image of God (Genesis 1:26, 27) and therefore have great worth in God’s
sight, and

**Whereas**, God’s Word also indicates that human life is therefore of such

value that to destroy that life for any reason other than that which God
has prescribed, which is murder, is of such seriousness as to merit capital
punishment (Genesis 9:5, 6), and

<a id="ga47-p710"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=712 printed_page=710 -->

**Whereas**, God has clearly indicated in his Word that murder is a sin against

him, according to the moral law applicable to all times and
circumstances, as codified in the tablets of stone known as the Ten
Commandments (Exodus 20:13; Deuteronomy 5:17), and

**Whereas**, the sin of abortion continues to be a common practice in our

culture despite political and religious efforts to outlaw, restrict, and
prevent it, and

**Whereas**, our _Confession of Faith_ indicates that it is proper and necessary

for synods, “…by way of humble petition in cases extraordinary…”
( _WCF_ 31:4) to address matters which pertain to the civil magistrate, and

**Whereas**, both the legalization of the murder of a helpless class, resulting in

the loss of more than 51 million lives, and the forced subsidization of
such by individuals opposed to it, constitute such an extraordinary case,

**Therefore, be it resolved** that the General Assembly of the Presbyterian

Church in America officially, and on the basis of the inspired and
authoritative Word of God, declare the following:

We reaffirm our denomination’s commitment on this pertinent,
prevalent issue regarding the sanctity of life and our stance against
abortion in particular. We commend our denominational overtures,
particularly

1979 – Overture 7 - Revise Assembly’s statement on abortion
1980 – Overture 12 - Reaffirm original statement on abortion
1984 – Overture 14 - Adopt statement that a memorial service is

Biblical and appropriate in cases of miscarriage
1989 – Overture 17 - Oppose research on transplantation of fetal

tissue

1993 – Overture 25 - Protection of Life of the Unborn
1993 – Overture 41 - Exhort SBC to Continue Strong Stand Against

Abortion
1999 – Overture 27 - Reaffirm the sanctity of life
2010 – Overture 28 - Sanctity of Life Resolution
2014 – Overture 43 - Expression of Support for Sanctity of Life and

Marriage

The content of these preceding overtures, which span years, and can be
difficult to find, are compiled for easy reference in the Attachment to
this overture, which may be found beginning on p. 718.

_Adopted by Hills and Plains Presbytery at its stated meeting, May 4, 2019_
_Attested by /s/ RE Bill Boyd, stated clerk_

<a id="ga47-p711"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=713 printed_page=711 -->

##### OVERTURE 47 from Missouri Presbytery (to AC, OC)

“Formation of a Study Committee on Domestic Abuse and Sexual
Assault” (Version 9)

**Whereas**, the sins of domestic abuse and sexual assault are expressly

forbidden (2 Timothy 3:2-5; Jude 7; Exodus 21:15; Deuteronomy 22:2527; also _WLC_ 135 and 139); and

**Whereas**, these sins are especially heinous because they are “against the

express letter of the law,” “break many commandments,” “admit of no
reparation,” and often involve various other aggravations ( _WLC_ 151);
and

**Whereas**, these sins are grievously prevalent in our society; [21] and

**Whereas**, these sins often produce in the victims a complex response of

denial along with immense fear and shame that persist with varying
severity throughout life (e.g., 2 Samuel 13:1-22); and

**Whereas**, it is the responsibility of the elders of the church to exercise their

authority, severally and jointly, to shepherd those who are under their
care by guarding against such sinful behavior as much as possible, by
wisely caring for those victimized by the sins of others, and by
appropriately disciplining those guilty of such sins toward a full
repentance; and

**Whereas,** these sins have been a concerning subject of attention not only in

other denominations but even in the proceedings of the 46th General
Assembly; and

**Whereas**, the 42nd General Assembly previously exhorted the PCA and its

courts to respond boldly against one form of these sins, namely the
sexual abuse of children; and

**Whereas**, no standardized resources for pastoral care on these sins and their

complex consequences exist for officers, church courts, and agencies in
the PCA; and

**Whereas**, no clear guidelines for policy or practice with regard to these sins

exist for the officers, church courts, and agencies in the PCA that would
protect individuals and congregations; and

21 For example, the National Domestic Violence Hotline reports: “More than 1 in 3 women

(35.6%) and more than 1 in 4 men (28.5%) in the United States have experienced rape,
physical violence and/or stalking by an intimate partner in their lifetime” (available at
http://www.thehotline.org/resources/statistics/, accessed 6/15/18). For further sobering
statistics on sexual violence see the National Center for Injury Prevention and Controls
report entitled _Sexual Violence Surveillance: Uniform Definitions and Recommended Data_
_Elements_ (Atlanta: Center for Disease Control, 2014; available online at
http://www.cdc.gov/violenceprevention/pdf/sv_surveillance_definitionsl-2009-a.pdf).

<a id="ga47-p712"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=714 printed_page=712 -->

**Whereas**, the Rules of Discipline make no consideration for the unique

heinousness of these sins and their practical effect on victims;

**Therefore, be it resolved** that Missouri Presbytery hereby overtures the 47th

General Assembly of the PCA to form a committee to study:
(1) the nature of domestic abuse and sexual assault;
(2) the pastoral resources available for the care of survivors of these sins;
(3) the best practices and policy guidelines for elders, church courts, and

agencies for protecting against these sins and for responding to them;
(4) the possibility of changes to the Rules of Discipline that would

appropriately protect victims of these sins within a biblical process;
and

(5) the possible need for repentance as a church; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall be appointed by the

moderator, and shall include among its members or advisory members at
least two subject-matter experts and at least two women; and

**Be it further resolved** that the budget for the study committee be set at

$15,000/year and that funds be derived from gifts to the AC designated
for that purpose, with Missouri Presbytery committed to offer some
money; and

**Be it further resolved** that the committee shall present their findings and

recommendations for adoption by the 48th General Assembly.

_Adopted by Missouri Presbytery at its stated meeting on April 16, 2019_
_Attested by /s/ TE Timothy R. Butler, stated clerk_

##### OVERTURE 48 from the Session of Christ the King (to OC)

Presbyterian Church, Norman, Oklahoma

“Make a Stronger Statement regarding Abortion”
(Rejected by Hills and Plains Presbytery at its stated meeting
May 4, 2019 [ _RAO_ 11-10])

**Whereas**, the Word of God indicates that all human beings are created in the

image of God (Genesis 1:26, 27) and consequently have great worth in
God’s sight, and

**Whereas**, God’s Word also indicates that human life is therefore of such

value that to destroy that life for any reason other than that which God
has prescribed, which is murder, is of such seriousness as to merit capital
punishment (Genesis 9:5, 6), and

**Whereas**, God has clearly indicated in His Word that murder is a sin against

Him, according to the moral law applicable to all times and

<a id="ga47-p713"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=715 printed_page=713 -->

circumstances, as codified in the tablets of stone known as the Ten
Commandments (Exodus 20:13; Deuteronomy 5:17), and

**Whereas**, the Supreme Court decision known as Roe v. Wade, which struck

down laws prohibiting the practice of abortion of unborn children and
legalized abortion in every state in the United States of America, was
predicated upon a fraudulent case involving false testimony with respect
to the circumstances surrounding it, which circumstances thus wielded
powerfully emotional and insidiously persuasive, yet mythical, import,
and

**Whereas**, the fraudulent nature of the entire case was irrefutably verified by

testimony subsequent to the ruling by the plaintiff in the case, Norma
McCorvey, the so-called Jane Roe, such that crucial alleged facts
testified to in court regarding her need for an abortion and injuries
suffered by her due to her inability to obtain one were untrue (as
documented in the books _I_ _Am Roe: My Life, Roe V. Wade, and Freedom_
_of_ Choice and _Won by Love_ and the documentary film “I Was Wrong”),
and

**Whereas**, Ms. McCorvey spent much of the rest of her life seeking to correct

the record and advocate the pro-life cause, and

**Whereas**, in the course of such advocacy, Ms. McCorvey testified to the

manipulation and use of her by ideological radicals intent on promoting
the legalization of abortion by any means necessary, and

**Whereas**, her obviously relevant and groundbreaking testimony has been

largely ignored by both the mainstream media and our culture at large,
such that Roe v. Wade, as legal opinion, is unjustifiably and ignorantly
revered by many, and

**Whereas**, the practice of abortion and its promotion have long been linked

to the secular humanistic ideology undergirding the practice of eugenics,
which came to expression in the death camps of Nazi Germany, and
which was advocated in the writings of the founder (Margaret Sanger,
who publicly advocated the forced sterilization, abortion, and
elimination of whole races and classes of people) of the most prolific
dispenser of abortion today, Planned Parenthood, and

**Whereas**, recent video documentary evidence (made public by the Center

for Medical Progress in 2015, and validated by the investigation and
ruling of the United States Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals in January,
2019) has demonstrated that Planned Parenthood has sold body parts
obtained through abortion to those wishing to harvest human organs for
a variety of purposes, in violation of law, and

**Whereas**, certain lawmakers within the United States government have

persisted and now again succeeded in forcing American taxpayers,

<a id="ga47-p714"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=716 printed_page=714 -->

against their desires and beliefs, in multiple ways, to fund and undergird
these wicked practices through government subsidization of Planned
Parenthood and other less prominent abortion providers, and

**Whereas**, medical science has advanced to the point of demonstrating,

beyond a shadow of a doubt, that the fetus within a mother’s womb is a
living organism, distinct from the mother’s body (possessing distinct
DNA, etc.), and

**Whereas**, the notion that a living being changes into a different type of

organism by passing through a birth canal is untenable, both logically
and scientifically, and

**Whereas**, God’s Word refers to life in the womb as having personal and

human existence (e.g., Psalm 139:15, 16) and metes out capital
punishment for causing the death of an unborn child, confirming its
status as a human being created in the image of God (Exodus 21:22-25),
and

**Whereas**, the oft-cited cases of rape and incest do not justify the practice of

abortion, since the unborn child bears no responsibility for those sins and
is the one who is punished by losing his life through abortion, though
innocent, which is a gross injustice and which adds sin to sin, and

**Whereas**, there are no such things as “unwanted children,” adoption

agencies having waiting lists of years consisting of couples who
desperately want children and would gladly take those who are being
aborted, and

**Whereas**, the support services for pregnant women, including the unwed,

are so prolific as to render any woman contemplating an abortion utterly
unjustified, if not dishonest, in basing her decision on a lack of support
or ability to raise the child, and

**Whereas**, since Roe v. Wade, there have been more than 51 million murders

of human beings through abortion in the United States (an extremely
conservative figure, according to a comparison of information from the
Centers for Disease Control and the Guttmacher Institute), the
overwhelming majority of which have been perpetrated in order to avoid
responsibility, undergird promiscuity, assert sinful autonomy, or for
other depraved purposes (based on information obtained through
surveys by the Guttmacher Institute), and

**Whereas**, according to statistics available to the public, in the state of New

York there are now more abortions than live births, and

**Whereas**, God’s Word continually commands His people to do such as to

“open your mouth for the mute, for the rights of all who are destitute;
open your mouth, judge righteously, defend the rights of the poor and

<a id="ga47-p715"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=717 printed_page=715 -->

needy” (Proverbs 31:8, 9), and is full of such admonitions to speak for
and defend those who cannot speak for or defend themselves, and

**Whereas**, the foregoing demonstrates that the unborn are most certainly a

quintessential example of such a category of persons, and

**Whereas**, the recent bill passed by the New York legislature and signed into

law by its governor effectively legalizes not only abortion up until the
moment of birth, but also infanticide after birth at the sole discretion of
the mother, an act which was given a standing ovation by those present,
and

**Whereas**, actions such as these are beyond ghastly and are indicated in

Scripture to invite the severe judgment of God upon societies and nations
which permit such atrocities, and

**Whereas**, our _Confession of Faith_ indicates that it is proper and necessary

for synods, “…by way of humble petition in cases extraordinary…”
( _WCF_ 31:4) to address matters which pertain to the civil magistrate, and

**Whereas**, both the legalization of the murder of a helpless class, resulting in

the loss of more than 51 million lives, and the forced subsidization of
such by individuals opposed to it, constitute such an extraordinary case
by any reasonable and honest definition, and

**Whereas**, elders in the Church of the Lord Jesus Christ are shepherds and

watchmen, given the responsibility to warn its people of the solemn
judgment of God when their sin demands such, and

**Whereas**, the Scriptures declare that a failure to discharge this responsibility

faithfully, as in the case of the watchmen and shepherds of Israel,
disposes those shepherds and watchmen themselves to the awesome
judgment of God (Isaiah 56:9-12; Ezekiel 33:1-9; 34:1-10);

**Therefore**, be it resolved that the Session of Christ the King Presbyterian

Church, Norman, Oklahoma, hereby overture the 47 [th] General Assembly
of the Presbyterian Church in America and request that it officially, and
on the basis of the inspired and authoritative Word of God, declare the
following:

First, that all who participate in the practice of abortion, including
mothers, for any reason other than to save the physical life of the mother,
are guilty of complicity in heinous sin, and subject to the severe
judgment of God unless they repent and seek His forgiveness,

Second, that all such above persons are not beyond the grace of God, and
that forgiveness and healing are abundantly available to such as will
humble themselves in godly sorrow leading to sincere repentance and
submission to the revealed will of God in Scripture,

<a id="ga47-p716"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=718 printed_page=716 -->

Third, that sexual activity and pregnancy are inextricably linked
according to the design of God the Creator, rendering individuals
engaged in intentional sexual activity responsible for all of its results and
consequences, and

Fourth, that a woman’s body, in addition to being biologically distinct
from an unborn baby, is moreover not solely her own, to be regarded and
utilized purely in accordance with her own autonomous discretion, but
rather ultimately belongs to God the Creator, and is to be utilized strictly
in accordance with His revealed will, and

Fifth, that all professing Christians, especially those who labor in civil
government, are obligated to oppose the practice of abortion with all the
vehemence befitting the severity and urgency of the issue,

Sixth, that professing Christians who labor in civil government, who also
advocate and approve of the practice of abortion, should be disciplined
to the limit of church censure until and unless they repent of such
wickedness, and

Seventh, that the civil authorities in the United States of America, at all
levels, are hereby warned that they are inviting the temporal and
awesome judgment of God upon our nation in allowing this atrocity to
continue, and that they are urged to repent and exercise their God-given
function of restraining evil by protecting the rights of all people,
especially the powerless, and including the unborn, and

Eighth, that the churches of the Presbyterian Church in America are
urged to consider the inclusion of ministries to abortion clinics and
support of and cooperation with pro-life pregnancy resource agencies as
part of their diaconal programs, and

Ninth, that the churches of the Presbyterian Church in America are urged
to redouble their efforts in biblical instruction on sex, sexuality,
marriage, and family, and on God’s will concerning the interrelation of
and participation in all of these.

**Grounds:**

1. The previous overtures and statements approved by the General

Assembly, while salutary, do not speak explicitly and strongly enough to
the biblical specifics surrounding the issue.

<a id="ga47-p717"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=719 printed_page=717 -->

2. These previous overtures contain nothing affirming and declaring the

responsibility of the Church to discipline those who are sinning in this

area.

3. These previous overtures include little regarding the responsibility of

Christian political leaders to oppose abortion, or regarding their sin in
being complicit in approving or promoting it through various legislation,
and their consequent liability to church discipline. Inasmuch as there are
many professing Christians in the political arena who regard their
political activity and commitments as unaccountable to church discipline
and who thus feel confident in participating in the promotion of abortion
on demand and its funding with no spiritual consequences, this needs to
be addressed explicitly and officially by the Church, and such attention
is long overdue.
4. The scientific, historical, and moral mythology surrounding abortion and

its justification, including the deceptive and false context of the Roe V.
Wade decision, and the nature and history of Planned Parenthood, needs
to be officially and comprehensively addressed in the rationale for the
Church’s position. This has not been adequately accomplished in
previous overtures, and is the reason for the extensive “Whereas” section
of the proposed overture.
5. The secular humanistic culture concomitant with abortion on demand is

becoming more brazen and aggressive daily. This requires a forceful,
frank, and aggressive response from the Church. Abortion is an atrocity.
Language which mollifies portrayal of the extreme evil of its nature, or
which fails to speak accurately and honestly about the ramifications of
its practice, is a dereliction of the duty of ministers of the Word and
shepherds who are called to tell the sheep the truth, whatever
consequences that might entail. To water down the truth in an effort to
be “temperate” is in this context to unfaithful to our calling.
6. Sound and truly loving pastoral practice brings those who have sinned to

an awareness of the heinousness of their sin, leading to godly sorrow
which produces true repentance unto life. A too hasty application of
comfort to those who have sinned in this way makes such a process
impossible. Discretion and prudence are needed in the application of
truth to individual cases, discerning where a person is in the process of
repentance, but refraining from strong public statements and warnings
regarding the practice of abortion does not well serve those who are
grieving over their sin. Rather, true repentance joined with genuine faith
in the justifying grace of Christ should render such individuals the
strongest proponents of such language.

<a id="ga47-p718"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=720 printed_page=718 -->

**Attachment To Overture 46**

**(see p. 709)**

**Overtures to Previous General Assemblies**

**Regarding the Sanctity of Life**

**1979**
**15**, North Georgia, to B&O, 7-8, B, p. 33; 7-37, III, 3, p. 97, Affirmative as
amended, “Revise Assembly's statement on abortion” _[M6GA, p. 72]_

##### Overture 15. From the Presbytery of North Georgia

Whereas, the Sixth General Assembly was of one mind in its general

consideration of the abortion issue, but was divided on the question
whether abortion might be justified if deemed necessary to save a
woman’s life,

Therefore, be it resolved that North Georgia Presbytery request the General

Assembly to revise the Assembly’s statement on abortion ( _Minutes_, p.
72) to read as follows:

1. That because Scripture clearly affirms the sanctity of

human life and condemns its arbitrary destruction, we
affirm that the intentional killing of an unborn child
between conception and birth (abortion) is clearly a
violation of the Sixth Commandment in all cases except
possibly that in which it appears that no other remedy will
save the life of the mother. The Assembly is divided as to
whether this case would justify abortion.”

_Adopted unanimously at the Stated Winter Meeting of North Georgia Presbytery,_
_January 20, 1979._
_Attest: R. G. Valentine, Stated Clerk of North Georgia Presbytery_
Answered in the affirmative as amended. § 7-37, III, 3; p. 97.

**1979**
**18**, Eastern Carolina, to B&O, 7-8, B, p. 33; 7-37, III, 3, p. 97, Answered by
statement; refer to Overture 15, “Amend 1978 statement on abortion”

**1979**
**33,** Mid-Atlantic, to B&O, 7-8, B, p. 34; 7-37, III, 3, p. 97, Answered as
amended; refer to Overture 15,

<a id="ga47-p719"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=721 printed_page=719 -->

**1980**
_**12**_ _,_ Evangel, to B&O, 8-7, B, p. 33; 8-69, III, 2, p. 97, Affirmative as
amended, “Reaffirm original statement” [ _M6GA_ ] on abortion

##### Overture 12: From Evangel Presbytery

Whereas, the General Assembly, as a court of jesus Christ, should speak

with a united voice in affirming the sanctity of human life under the
protection of the Sixth Commandment,

Therefore Be It Resolved, that Evangel Presbytery overture the Eighth

General Assembly to reaffirm its original statement on abortion: "That
because Scripture clearly affirms the sanctity of human life and
condemns its arbitrary destruction, we affirm that the intentional killing
of an unborn child between conception and birth, for any reason at any
time, is clearly a violation of the Sixth Commandment. "

Added to this statement are the following affirmations: "Concerning crisis
pregnancies in which it appears that the premature removal of the unborn
child from the mother is a necessary consequence of medical procedures
essential to preserving the mother's life, we affirm that: The Sixth
Commandment not only forbids the taking of innocent life for whatever
reason, but also requires the preserving of innocent life wherever possible,
The life of the mother as well as her child comes under the full protection of
the Sixth Commandment and all moral medical wisdom and skill must be

used to preserve her life as well as the life of her child.
_Attest: C. Gene Pjrks Stated Clerk_

To say that we are ever placed in the position of choosing between two sins
(killing the child or neglecting the preservation of the mother's life) is to
surrender our belief in the Sovereign overruling providence of our All-wise
and All-holy God (1 Cor. 10:13) and to allege that we must ' do evil that good
may come” . . a philosophy clearly condemned by Scripture.

An exception is not made even in the extremely rare case in which in the
judgment of competent medical authorities, the unborn child’s continuing
presence inside the mother's body will necessarily lead to the mother's death,
the premature removal of the unborn child may be justified, provided that all
medical wisdom, judgment, and skill are used to preserve the life of the child
as well as the life of the mother. This premature removal of the unborn child
shall be at that juncture of time where the greatest possibility for recovery is
indicated for both mother and child. If life is lost in such a case, and the death
occurs not out of criminal negligence, but merely out of the limitations of

<a id="ga47-p720"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=722 printed_page=720 -->

human knowledge and skill, all has been done morally that could be done. If
we cannot save both lives, we are nevertheless morally bound to save the life
we can. "

_Adopted this 22nd day of January, 1980, in meeting at the Oak Park_
_Presbyterian Church, Montgomery, Alabama. Answered in the affirmative_
_as amended. § 8-69, III, 2; p. 97._

**1980**

_**27**_ _,_ Ascension, to B&O, 8-7, B, p. 33; 8-69, III, 3, p. 98, Refer to Overture
12, “Affirm action of Sixth GA re: abortion”

**1984**
**09** _,_ Central Florida, to CJB, 12-10, B, p. 48; 12-53, II, 32, p. 134, Refer to
Overture 14, “In re: Services for worship for infants who do not survive
premature birth”

**1984**
**14** _,_ Westminster, to B&O, 12-10, B, p. 42; 12-31, III, 3, p. 102, Affirmative
as amended, “Adopt statement that a memorial service is Biblical and
appropriate in cases of miscarriage”

##### Overture 14 : From the Presbytery of Westminster

Whereas, the Presbyterian Church in America, in conformity with the

teaching of Scripture, has affirmed that human life begins at conception
and continues both before and after birth (Report of the Ad-Interim
Committee on Abortion, adopted by the Sixth General Assembly); and

Whereas, miscarriage, as well as abortion, most often results in the death of

a human being; and

Whereas, the loss of the life of an unborn child by miscarriage is often a

definite source of grief and sorrow to the parents of the child; and

Whereas, funeral and memorial services are regularly conducted for the

worship of God and the consolation of the living at the death of a loved
one; and

Whereas, it has not generally been the practice in our churches to conduct

memorial services at the time of miscarriages in which there are no
physical remains of the child to be buried; and

Whereas, this issue which has long been neglected by the Church of Christ

and which now, more than ever, should be addressed by the church in
the light of Scripture;

<a id="ga47-p721"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=723 printed_page=721 -->

Therefore, Westminster Presbytery respectfully overtures the Twelfth

General Assembly to adopt the following statement: “In view of the
Biblical teaching that life begins at conception, a memorial service,
given to the glory of God and for the comfort of the bereaved, is Biblical
and therefore appropriate in regard to miscarriage, and should be
encouraged, regardless of the existence of physical remains available for
burial.”

_Attest: Larry E. Ball, Stated Clerk_
_Answered in the affirmative as amended, 12-31, III, 3, p. 102._

**1989**
**23,** Tennessee Valley, to B&O, 17-91, II, 5, p. 178, “Oppose research on
transplantation of fetal tissue”

##### Overture 23 from Tennessee Valley Presbytery, "Oppose Research on

Transplantation of Fetal Tissue"

Whereas National Institutes of Health (NIH), a federal agency under the

Department of Health and Human Services is responsible for supporting
the majority of health sciences research, and

Whereas NIH is in the process of deciding whether to lift the moratorium on

the funding of fetal tissue research, and

Whereas NIH has accepted the unanimous recommendation from the

Advisory Council to the Director, which states, according to the January
1989 issue of the _FASEB Newsletter_ (the official newsletter of the
Federation of American Societies of Experimental Biology), "The
federal government should resume funding research on the therapeutic
transplantation of fetal tissues from elective abortions as treatment for
diabetes and Parkinson's disease," and

Whereas we, as members of the evangelical, Bible-believing denomination,

The Presbyterian Church in America, believe that elective abortions are
against the teaching of the Bible, and

Whereas we believe that the arguments to use fetal tissue obtained from

elective abortions would be a seductive, though perhaps unintended, way
to convince the general public that elective abortions are in the public
interest if the tissues so obtained may be used for transplantations to
relieve human suffering,

Now therefore be it resolved that the Tennessee Valley Presbytery

respectfully overture the 17th General Assembly of the Presbyterian
Church in America (PCA) to express its moral conviction to the

<a id="ga47-p722"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=724 printed_page=722 -->

executive and legislative branches of the federal government that such
research using fetal tissue from _elective_ abortions should not be allowed,
and

Be it further resolved that the General Assembly inform our congregations

of these issues and encourage all members of the PCA to write their
congressmen and senators informing them of their opposition.

_Adopted at the Spring Stated Meeting of the Tennessee Valley Presbytery_
_Attest: J. Render Caines, Stated Clerk_

**1993**
**39**, Rocky Mountain, to B&O, 21-56, III, 25, p. 160, “Protection of Life of
the Unborn”

**25.** That Overture 39 from Rocky Mountain Presbytery be answered in the

affirmative as amended. _Adopted_

##### Overture 39 From Rocky Mountain Presbytery

"Protection of Life of the Unborn"

Whereas, Scripture teaches us the sacredness of human life (Genesis 9:6-7);

and Whereas, Scripture teaches that human life begins at conception
(Psalm 139:13-16) with God "having fashioned us in our mother's
womb"; and Whereas, since 1973 a monstrous blight of abortion on
demand has come upon our nation through the Supreme Court decision
of _Roe_ v. _Wade-,_ and

Whereas, the Christian Church has had a long history of opposition to

abortion (e.g., _Didache,_ 2.2); and

Whereas, the PCA has taken a consistent Christian position in opposition to

abortion on demand (see e.g., _M6GA,_ Appendix O, pp. 270-285, as well
as subsequent GAs to the 6th GA, that passed resolutions in opposition
to abortion); and

Whereas, the Christian Church in this land is free to lawfully appeal and

plead to her rulers for godly laws and precepts to be enacted, and
contrariwise, to urge the repeal of ungodly laws; and

Whereas, President Clinton has enacted executive orders to further endanger

the safety and sanctity of the unborn; and

Whereas, President Clinton has taken a public stand approving abortion on

demand, despite the Christian Church's (including President Clinton's
own Church) long and honorable support of the unborn;

Therefore, Be it Resolved that the 21st General Assembly of the Presbyterian

Church in America prayerfully and respectfully urge President Clinton

<a id="ga47-p723"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=725 printed_page=723 -->

to do all in his power as Chief Executive of the United States of America
(having been placed in that position by God, Rom. 13:1-7) to protect the
life of the unborn, for ultimately he must answer to Almighty God for
the responsibilities to which he has been entrusted (2 Corinthians 5:10)
and that the Stated Clerk of the PCA communicate this to the President

by means of this overture and the PCA's position paper on abortion."

_Adopted at Spring Stated Meeting of Rocky Mountain Presbytery, April 22, 1993._
_Attested by: /s/ Lyle E. Lagasse Stated Clerk_

**1993**
**41** _,_ South Texas, to B&O, 21-56, III, 27, p. 161, “Exhort SBC to continue
strong stand against abortion”

**27.** That Overture 41, from South Texas Presbytery be answered in the
affirmative as amended: _Adopted_

##### OVERTURE 41 from the Presbytery of South Texas

"Exhort SBC to Continue Strong Stand Against Abortion" _Adopted_

Whereas, the Bible declares that life begins at conception and therefore, that

abortion is murder,

Whereas, the present administration of the federal government supports pro
abortion positions and practices;

Whereas, the Southern Baptist Convention has on numerous prior occasions

taken a stance in opposition to abortion;

Therefore Let it Be Resolved that the General Assembly of the Presbyterian

Church in America exhorts our brothers in the Southern Baptist
Convention to continue to take a Scriptural position concerning the value
of human life and the atrocity of abortion and the responsibility of all
Christian leaders to support human life issues.

_Adopted by the Presbytery of South Texas on April 24,1993._
_Attested by: /s/ Irvin M. May Jr., Stated Clerk_

**1999**
**27,** Philadelphia, to B&O, 27-44, III, 14, p. 175, Affirmative as amended,
“Reaffirm the sanctity of life”

<a id="ga47-p724"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=726 printed_page=724 -->

That **OVERTURE 27** from the Presbytery of Philadelphia, “Reaffirm the

Sanctity of Life,” be answered with this substitute: _Adopted as amended_

The 27 [th] General Assembly of the PCA reaffirms with united voice the

sanctity of human life under the protection of the Sixth Commandment.
And to that end,

1. The Stated Clerk is requested to do the following:

a. Remind all the churches and presbyteries of the continuing

availability of its published report on the sanctity of Human Life
adopted by the Sixth General Assembly.
b. That his letter to the churches regarding the actions of the 27th

General Assembly repeat recommendations 2, 3, 5, 6, and 8
urging our churches and presbyteries to seek God’s enablement
and grace to implement them in light of the continued tragedy of
abortion on demand in America.

c. Appoint someone to lead the Assembly in prayer for this issue

at this time.

d. Appoint a national PCA day of prayer and fasting for the ending

of abortion to be held at an appropriate day in the year AD 2000,
inviting NAE churches, our sister NAPARC churches and other
approved PCA ministries and agencies to join with us.
2. That each of the PCA Presbyteries be urged to establish a pro-life

committee to address the recommendations of the Sixth General

Assembly Report on the Sanctity of Human Life among its
constituent particular churches.

RE Robert Brown led the Assembly in prayer regarding the sanctity of life
issue.

NOTE: The following commissioners registered their negative note on
Recommendation 14, especially item 6 - TE David F. Coffin Jr ; TE J. Paul
Poynor, and TE Scott R. Wright

**2010**
**28**, South Florida, to OC, 38-54, III, 4, p. 352, Affirmative as amended,
“Sanctity of Life Resolution”

4. That **Overture 28** from South Florida Presbytery (“Sanctity of Life
Resolution”) be answered in the affirmative as amended as follows (strikethrough for deletions; underscore and bold for additions):

<a id="ga47-p725"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=727 printed_page=725 -->

Therefore be it resolved by the Presbyterian Church in America, that we will

encourage all our members to immediately begin to approach professing
Christians in all denominations:

To join with the entire Body of Christ for the protection of the unborn,
acting in solidarity with other denominations in prayer and obedience to
God, by whose sovereign might alone the scourge of legalized abortion
will be broken;

To offer pastoral and practical care for those individuals affected by
unplanned pregnancies or victimized by abortion;

To speak prophetically against the culture of death, confronting it in the
power of God’s truth;

To proclaim ~~hold our culture accountable before~~ the binding and
unchallengeable authority of God’s Holy Word over every man, woman,
institution, and nation, in every area of life;

To lovingly call offending parties to repentance in Jesus Christ, and
restoration by the Holy Spirit to new obedience;

To declare ~~proclaim~~ the authoritative truth of God, both to our own
members and in the public square, concerning God’s righteous demands
upon the judicial, legislative, and executive branches of our government
at every level.

The General Assembly instructs the Stated Clerk to communicate the
substance of this overture to each presbytery, session and teaching elder
of the PCA to further communicate to their congregations.

so that as amended, the resolution reads:

Therefore be it resolved by the Presbyterian Church in America, that we will

encourage all our members to immediately begin to approach professing
Christians in all denominations:

To join with the entire Body of Christ for the protection of the unborn,
acting in solidarity with other denominations in prayer and obedience to
God, by whose sovereign might alone the scourge of legalized abortion
will be broken;

<a id="ga47-p726"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=728 printed_page=726 -->

To offer pastoral and practical care for those individuals affected by
unplanned pregnancies or victimized by abortion;

To speak prophetically against the culture of death, confronting it in the
power of God’s truth;

To proclaim the binding and unchallengeable authority of God’s Holy
Word over every man, woman, institution, and nation, in every area of
life;

To lovingly call offending parties to repentance in Jesus Christ, and
restoration by the Holy Spirit to new obedience;

To declare the authoritative truth of God, both to our own members and
in the public square, concerning God’s righteous demands upon the
judicial, legislative, and executive branches of our government at every
level.

The General Assembly instructs the Stated Clerk to communicate the
substance of this overture to each presbytery, session and teaching elder
of the PCA to further communicate to their congregations.” _Adopted_

Grounds:

a) The Holy Spirit, speaking infallibly in Scripture, declares that human

life is sacred, and that it begins at conception.
b) This authoritative declaration by Almighty God makes the protection

of all human life from the moment of conception to the moment of
birth an inescapable moral duty for all men.
c) The taking of the life of an unborn person is a violation of the sixth

commandment, “Thou shall not murder," and is in fact premeditated
murder.

d) A considerable number of our elected and appointed government

leaders want to make and keep legal the killing of the unborn for any
and every reason.
e) This assault on the unborn calls for a unified effort by Christians of

all denominations to protect the unborn child from the moment of
conception to the moment of birth and afterwards.
f) Each and every person will one day stand before our sovereign God

to give an account for his or her life, and whether or not that life was
lived under the Lordship of Jesus Christ, the Lord of life.

<a id="ga47-p727"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=729 printed_page=727 -->

##### Overture 28 from the South Florida Presbytery “Sanctity of Life Resolution”

Whereas the Holy Spirit, speaking infallibly in Scripture, declares that

human life is sacred, and that it begins at conception; and

Whereas this authoritative declaration by Almighty God makes the

protection of all human life from the moment of conception to the
moment of birth an inescapable moral duty for all men; and

Whereas the taking of the life of an unborn person is a violation of the sixth

commandment, “Thou shall not murder," and is in fact premeditated
murder; and

Whereas a considerable number of our elected and appointed government

leaders want to make and keep legal the killing of the unborn for any and
every reason; and

Whereas this assault on the unborn calls for a unified effort by Christians of

all dominations to protect the unborn child from the moment of
conception to the moment of birth and afterwards; and

Whereas each and every person will one day stand before our sovereign God

to give an account for his or her life, and whether or not that life was
lived under the Lordship of Jesus Christ, the Lord of life;

Therefore be it resolved by the Presbyterian Church in America, that we will

encourage all our members to immediately begin to approach professing
Christians in all denominations:

To join with the entire Body of Christ for the protection of the unborn,
acting in solidarity with other denominations in prayer and obedience to
God, by whose sovereign might alone the scourge of legalized abortion
will be broken;

To speak prophetically against the culture of death, confronting it in the
power of God’s truth;

To hold our culture accountable before the binding and unchallengeable
authority of God’s Holy Word over every man, woman, institution, and
nation, in every area of life;

To lovingly call offending parties to repentance in Jesus Christ, and
restoration by the Holy Spirit to new obedience;

To proclaim the authoritative truth of God, both to our own members and
in the public square, concerning God’s righteous demands upon the
judicial, legislative, and executive branches of our government at every
level.

<a id="ga47-p728"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=730 printed_page=728 -->

_Adopted by the South Florida Presbytery at its stated meeting, April 20, 2010_
_Attested by /s/ TE William N. Sofield, stated clerk_

**2014**
**43**, Savannah River, to OC, 57, 66, 850, Negative, “Expression of Support
for Sanctity of Life and Marriage”

##### Overture 43 from Savannah River Presbytery (to OC) “Expression of

Support for Sanctity of Life and Marriage”

Whereas the Bible states that when there is a conflict between God's Law and

human law, we must obey God rather than men (Acts 5:29), and Whereas
the First Amendment of the U.S. Constitution specifically prohibits
Congress from establishing any law "prohibiting the free exercise of
religion," and

Whereas the environment for religious freedom worsens day by day in the

United States, and

Whereas some Christians today – including employers, chaplains, and others,

must choose between being faithful to their Biblical convictions
regarding the sanctity of human life and the sanctity of marriage, or face
punitive federal government penalties, and

Whereas God calls His Church to bear prophetic witness to His Truth in

human society, and

Whereas the Presbyterian Church in America, of which the Savannah River

Presbytery is a member, is on record regarding the sanctity of human life
and of marriage in accordance with that Truth; therefore

Be it resolved that Savannah River Presbytery hereby go on record

expressing its gratitude to and prayers for corporations, public and
private for-profit and non-profit entities, chaplains and others who have
taken a stand for the sanctity of human life, and to the Christian men and
women in private businesses facing fines, penalties, and ostracism for
declining out of religious conviction to provide their services for samesex marriage ceremonies, and

Be it further resolved that Savannah River Presbytery call upon the 42 [nd]

General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America to adopt this
resolution.

_Adopted by Savannah River Presbytery at its Stated Meeting, April 15, 2014_
_Attested by/s/ RE William L Hatcher, stated clerk_

<a id="ga47-p729"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=731 printed_page=729 -->

#### **APPENDIX W** **COMMUNICATIONS** **TO THE FORTY-SEVENTH GENERAL ASSEMBLY** **OF THE PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH IN AMERICA**

**COMMUNICATION #1**
From the NAE (National Association of Evangelicals)

National Association of Evangelicals
PO Box 23269, Washington, D.C. 20026
February 18, 2019

General Assembly
Presbyterian Church in America
1700 North Brown Road, Suite 105
Lawrenceville, GA 30043

Dear Ruling and Teaching Elders of the PCA,

Greetings and God’s blessings in the Name of Jesus Christ as you gather this
June for the 47 [th] General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America.

Thank you for your faithfulness to God, Scripture and the historic orthodox
tenets of our Christian faith. God has abundantly blessed you in so many
ways including the significant growth of the PCA since your founding in
1973. The influence of your churches, schools, leaders and many ministries
extends far beyond the PCA to bless us all and we are grateful.

Your membership in the National Association of Evangelicals has long
contributed to the significant and ongoing influence you have on our forty
denominations and many evangelical organizations and ministries. PCA
leaders are multiple and exemplary on the NAE board and staff. You evidence
a fulfillment of the description on the PCA website that ”We long to join in
cooperative ministry with other parts of the universal church to pursue God’s
global mission.” Your engagement with other evangelicals is well described
on the first page when searching PCA on the internet: “The PCA is Reformed
in theology, Presbyterian in government, and active in missions. It is
characterized by a blend of Reformed practice and broad evangelicalism.”

The National Association of Evangelicals celebrates and blesses the PCA
with gratitude to our Lord for who you are and all you do as fellow disciples
of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Sincerely, in Christ
/s/ Leith Anderson, President

<a id="ga47-p730"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=732 printed_page=730 -->

**COMMUNICATION 2**

From The Reformed Church of Quebec
_Église réformée du Québec_
Interchurch Committee

May 14, 2019
Fraternal Greetings to the 47 [th] General Assembly
of the Presbyterian Church in America

Dear fathers and brothers in Christ,

Our Saviour calls us to be the lights of the world. We are thankful to be that
light amongst the eight million people living in the province of Quebec,
Canada. The _Église réformée du Québec_ (ERQ) is the _only_ French-speaking
Reformed confessional witness in the province of Quebec. The ERQ is
composed of a total of 360 souls worshiping in five local congregations. Our
witness is small, like a nightlight, but by God’s grace shining brightly.

Our light has been shining in many ways, particularly through Reformed
literature. In the French-speaking world, there is a growing interest in
Reformed theology and practice. Best sellers include books written by
pastors Kevin DeYoung, Tim Keller, John Piper, and R.C. Sproul. We are
feeding that hunger for solid, Reformed teaching.

For instance, Pastor Paulin Bédard writes, translates and prepares Reformed
instructional material for publication on the website, Ressources chrétiennes.
More than 3,700 articles are available free of charge to pastors and Christians
throughout the French-speaking world, particularly in developing countries
in French-speaking Africa.

Our congregation in Quebec City is publishing several books under the
banner _Éditions La Rochelle_, the name of the first French confession penned
by John Calvin. Publications include French translations of John Sartelle’s
booklet, _What Christian Parents Should Know about Infant Baptism;_ Calvin
Cumming’s _The Covenant of Grace;_ Louis Berkhof’s _A Summary of Christian_
_Doctrine;_ Mark Jones’ _Knowing Christ;_ and O. Palmer Robertson’s _The Christ_
_of the Covenant._ In celebration of the 400 [th] anniversary of the synod of Dort,
we published John Benton’s _Evangelistic Calvinism_ and Paulin Bédard’s _Le_
_solide fondement du salut,_ an in-depth study of the Canons of Dort.

Please pray for the Reformed witness in the province of Quebec, Canada as
the Lord raises up workers for the harvest. Our synod recently granted
licensure to two brothers, Blaise Tsabang and George-Émile Durand, even

<a id="ga47-p731"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=47 pdf_page=733 printed_page=731 -->

as they complete their studies at Farel Reformed Theological Seminary. The
seminary is directed by PCA missionary, John Garnet Zoellner, who has
faithfully served the Reformed Church in Quebec for more than thirty years.

Finally, even as you partner with us in the Quebec mission, we are seeking
to be missional. Our Montreal congregation is sponsoring a Syrian refugee
family. Our Quebec City congregation is sending a missionary worker to
Lyon, France to work as an administrator of a Christian radio network. Our
members, who are a minority of minorities – less than one percent of the
Quebec population profess an evangelical faith – seek to be light in the world
through their good works and their word of testimony. In a world increasingly
secular, individualistic, materialistic, hedonistic – in short, a world increasingly
dark, our nightlight shines more distinctly.

We pray for the Lord’s blessing upon your assembly, its deliberations and its
decisions. May the Lord continue to advance the reign of the Lord Jesus Christ
through your faithful witness.

With brotherly affection,

Ben Westerveld

Pastor

For the Interchurch Committee of the ERQ

Contact information:

Rev. Ben Westerveld

President, Interchurch Committee of the ERQ
844, rue de Contrecoeur
Québec (Québec) CANADA
G1X 2X8

ww.erq.qc.ca
[Pasteur-Bernard@erq.qc.ca](mailto:Pasteur-Bernard@erq.qc.ca)
(418) 659-7943

**Pastor:** Then I looked, and I heard around the throne and the living
